item.title: Upcoming news
A Historical Look at The American Legion Weekly and Its 1925 Covers
item.description: First published on July 4, 1919, The American Legion Weekly served as the forerunner to today’s American Legion Magazine. It provided an essential connection for veterans returning from World War I, offering guidance, social support, and a sense of purpose during their transition to civilian life. The publication played a key role in shaping the early identity of the American Legion, focusing on patriotism, veteran rehabilitation, and promoting '100 percent Americanism.' Beyond its advocacy work, the magazine is celebrated for its vivid illustrations and humorous cartoons, which depicted the daily lives, challenges, and camaraderie of former soldiers known as 'doughboys.' By blending serious topics like the early foundations of the GI Bill with lighthearted cultural glimpses of post-war America, the Weekly became a historical record of a generation striving to rebuild society after the war. The article highlights a collection of 30 vintage covers from 1925, showcasing the art, humor, a

item.title: Closing Arguments in OpenAI Trial Highlight Disputes Between Musk and Altman Over Company Conduct
item.description: The trial examining whether OpenAI and its executives misused the nonprofit for personal gain is nearing its conclusion in Oakland, California. Elon Musk's lawyer, Steven Molo, questioned the credibility of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, arguing that five witnesses testified to Altman being untrustworthy. Musk's team also accused OpenAI of prioritizing investor profits over AI safety, criticizing co-founder Greg Brockman's substantial personal stake. Microsoft was also implicated, with Musk's lawyers claiming the company enabled OpenAI's actions. OpenAI's legal team countered that Musk was aware of the organization's fundraising needs and had a personal interest in controlling the company, pointing to his past attempts to integrate OpenAI with Tesla and his creation of xAI. They argued Musk's lawsuit, filed in August 2024, was too late given his prior knowledge of OpenAI's growth plans, and challenged claims that he did not review key investment documents. Both sides presented closing argument

item.title: Political Analysts Clash Over U.S.-China Relations During CNN Debate
item.description: During a Thursday evening segment on CNN's 'NewsNight,' a heated debate erupted between political analysts John Avlon and Joe Borelli over U.S.-China relations. The discussion escalated after Borelli, a former Republican lawmaker, defended former President Donald Trump's approach to China, suggesting Trump was more effective because Xi Jinping had not directly threatened to take over Taiwan as he allegedly did to President Joe Biden. Avlon, a former Democratic candidate, strongly disagreed and pointed out that Biden had actually been criticized for taking a tough stance on China and Taiwan. The exchange quickly became contentious, with Avlon exclaiming, 'Oh my god!' and urging Borelli to fact-check his claims. Borelli insisted he had already looked into Biden's actions, leading to further back-and-forth shouting. The segment highlighted partisan tensions surrounding U.S.-China foreign policy and the tendency for discussions to devolve into personal attacks and cross-talk rather than su

item.title: Christopher Walken's Early Life and Career as a Child Actor in the 1950s
item.description: Before becoming an iconic actor known for his unique delivery and eccentric roles, Christopher Walken, originally known as Ronnie Walken, had a prolific career as a child actor in the 1950s. Born in 1943 in Astoria, Queens, he grew up with two brothers, Glenn and Kenneth, and was introduced to show business largely through his mother’s aspirations. Ronnie began modeling for catalogues around age seven and quickly transitioned to television. By age ten, he appeared in a sketch with Jerry Lewis and Dean Martin, which inspired him to take acting seriously. He played Kevin Acton in the sitcom Wonderful John Acton in 1953 and, along with his brother Glenn, originated the role of Michael Bauer on the soap opera The Guiding Light from 1954 to 1956. His style was influenced in his early teens by Elvis Presley, shaping a hairstyle that would remain iconic throughout his career. Outside of acting, Ronnie helped in the family business, Walken’s Bakery, run by his father, Paul. In 1964, he adopted

item.title: Key Legal Issues in Elon Musk’s Lawsuit Against OpenAI Co-Founders
item.description: Nine jurors in California are deliberating on Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI cofounders, including Sam Altman, and Microsoft. The case centers on whether Musk's donations to OpenAI were misused, and whether the organization and its for-profit arm enriched themselves unjustly. Musk alleges breach of charitable trust, claiming his contributions were intended to support AI development for public benefit, not private gain, especially after Microsoft’s $10 billion investment in 2023. He also accuses Microsoft of aiding and abetting this breach by being aware of donation restrictions. OpenAI counters with defenses including statute of limitations, claiming any potential harm occurred before the deadlines; unreasonable delay, arguing Musk filed the lawsuit years later after OpenAI’s successes; and unclean hands, highlighting Musk’s own competing AI projects and attempts to control OpenAI. Evidence presented suggests Musk’s donations were largely used as intended before 2021 and that OpenA

item.title: Street Photography Captures San Francisco Women's Fashion in the Early 1970s
item.description: The early 1970s in San Francisco were a time of rich cultural and fashion experimentation, with streets becoming a canvas for personal expression. Women’s fashion reflected a blend of bohemian influence, vintage inspiration, and emerging trends. Tie-dye shirts, Mexican peasant blouses, embroidered tops, ponchos, and military surplus items were common, paired with bell-bottoms, frayed denim, maxi dresses, and flowing skirts. Bold prints, Indian and Native American patterns, and vivid florals dominated the visual landscape. Accessories such as handcrafted jewelry, chokers, natural materials, headbands, floppy hats, and Birkenstocks emphasized individuality and the era’s unisex approach. Alongside the bohemian style, more tailored, polished looks gained traction, inspired by 1940s Hollywood glamour. Mini skirts, hot pants with tight tops, wide-leg trousers, blazers, and pastel tones created a structured aesthetic, gradually transitioning to warmer earthy colors after 1973. Knitwear emerge

item.title: Biographer raises concerns over Trump's late-night social media activity
item.description: President Donald Trump’s frequent late-night posts on Truth Social have raised concerns from mental health experts, according to biographer Michael Wolff. Since returning to office in January, Trump has made over 8,000 posts, often during hours when most people are asleep. Wolff highlighted that these posts contain claims such as accusing former President Barack Obama of treason and repeatedly asserting that the 2020 election was stolen, despite the lack of evidence in over 60 court cases. Wolff characterized these postings as erratic, irrational, and detached from reality, suggesting that if a family member exhibited similar behavior, it would be considered a mental health crisis. He also expressed concern that mainstream media has given minimal attention to the president's late-night posting habits, arguing that the nature of the content warrants greater scrutiny. The pattern suggests a potentially troubling trend in social media use by the president, raising questions about the impa

item.title: Ewan McGregor’s Early Career on the Set of Shallow Grave (1994)
item.description: In 1994, a young Ewan McGregor, at just 22 years old, starred in 'Shallow Grave,' a film that marked the beginning of a key creative partnership with director Danny Boyle and producer Andrew Macdonald. McGregor underwent a dramatic transformation for his role as Alex Law, adopting a sleek, mod-inspired style with slicked-back hair, dark turtlenecks, and an oversized coat. The on-screen tension and chemistry among the three leads—McGregor, Christopher Eccleston, and Kerry Fox—were amplified by their contrasting acting approaches. While McGregor and Fox embraced spontaneity, Eccleston stayed Method-oriented, creating a natural friction that enriched the performances. Filming mainly took place in a Glasgow warehouse, with a purpose-built, claustrophobic flat designed on a limited budget of roughly £1 million. The confined set, trapdoors, and attic spaces contributed to the frantic pace of the performances. Director Danny Boyle fostered a high-energy environment by playing music on set, a

item.title: A Tribute to Liliane Montevecchi: Icon of Stage and Screen
item.description: Liliane Montevecchi (1932–2018) was an extraordinary performer whose career spanned both European cabaret and Hollywood cinema. Beginning as a prima ballerina, she quickly became a star at Paris’s legendary Folies Bergère, captivating audiences with her grace and charisma. In the 1950s, Montevecchi transitioned to Hollywood, appearing in films like The Young Lions and How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days. Despite her success on screen, it was Broadway where she achieved legendary status, earning a Tony Award for her performance in Nine. Known for her sophisticated charm, expressive eyes, and athletic grace, Montevecchi was celebrated as a refined chanteuse and a magnetic stage presence. Even later in life, she remained an enduring symbol of elegance and vitality, inspiring generations of performers. This article features a curated collection of photos showcasing her youthful beauty and captivating persona, reflecting her journey from rising star to timeless icon.

item.title: Analysis of President Trump's Recent Social Media Activity and Its Implications
item.description: Over a roughly 21-hour period from Monday night into Tuesday morning, President Donald Trump posted or reposted 77 times on his Truth Social account, with more than 50 posts occurring in just over an hour. The posts included a range of baseless claims and conspiracy theories, such as alleging that former President Barack Obama attempted a coup against him during the 2016 election and that voting machines were manipulated in 2020 to cause his loss to President Joe Biden. Other posts targeted Democratic figures, including House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, using doctored imagery and personal attacks. Experts, including more than 30 medical professionals, have raised concerns over Trump’s mental fitness, suggesting that his behavior reflects a serious decline rather than isolated lapses or political theater. Observers note that Trump’s frequent social media posting contrasts with typical usage patterns, as average individuals spend about two and a half hours on social media daily, whi

item.title: A Detailed Look at the 1960 Jaguar XK150 Drophead Coupe
item.description: The 1960 Jaguar XK150 Drophead Coupe represents the final evolution of Jaguar's iconic XK series, combining elegance, performance, and innovation. Introduced as the most refined grand tourer of its era, the XK150 featured a sleek, modern design with a one-piece wraparound windshield and a widened bonnet, giving it a striking presence on the road. Notably, it was among the first production cars to come standard with four-wheel Dunlop disc brakes, showcasing Jaguar's racing expertise and commitment to safety. The vehicle was available with either a 3.4L or 3.8L straight-six engine, offering a balance of speed and reliability. The Drophead Coupe provided both luxurious comfort and open-air freedom, with a lined canvas top and a walnut-veneer dashboard adding to its sophisticated appeal. Today, the 1960 Jaguar XK150 Drophead Coupe remains highly prized by collectors and enthusiasts, admired for its timeless design, advanced engineering, and historical significance in post-war British autom

item.title: Senate Parliamentarian Blocks Republican Immigration Funding Proposal
item.description: Republicans faced a major setback in the Senate as Parliamentarian Elizabeth MacDonough blocked key parts of a proposed immigration enforcement funding package. The package, aimed at fully funding Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and Border Patrol following a prolonged Department of Homeland Security shutdown, was halted because it violated Senate rules. MacDonough cited the Byrd Rule, noting that several sections required a 60-vote threshold instead of a simple majority due to inappropriate allocations outside Homeland Security responsibilities. Specifically, $71.7 billion proposed for Customs and Border Protection and $2.5 billion for other DHS activities, including programs involving migrant children, were found to conflict with legal frameworks. Senate Majority Leader John Thune and Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham will need to revise the package and remove or adjust these provisions before bringing it to a floor vote. This decision represents a significant challenge

item.title: Jannik Sinner Achieves Record Winning Streak at 2026 Italian Open ATP Masters 1000
item.description: Due to restricted access to the original article, detailed reporting is unavailable. However, based on the title, the key highlight is that Italian tennis player Jannik Sinner achieved a record-breaking streak of consecutive wins in ATP Masters 1000 tournaments during the 2026 Italian Open. This milestone underscores Sinner's consistency and rising prominence in professional tennis. His performance in this prestigious tournament likely involved defeating top-ranked players and showcasing a combination of technical skill, mental resilience, and strategic gameplay. Setting such a record in ATP Masters 1000 events places him among the elite athletes in the sport, further enhancing his career trajectory and reputation within the global tennis community. Fans and analysts are expected to follow his progress closely in upcoming tournaments, as maintaining such a streak requires exceptional focus and fitness. This achievement also highlights Italy's growing impact on the international tennis

item.title: Democrats miss key vote on Trump's Iran war powers amid absences
item.description: Several Democratic lawmakers were absent during a critical House vote regarding President Donald Trump's war powers in Iran, prompting concern among party leadership. Axios reported that the absence of even a few members could have affected the outcome, highlighting the narrow margin in the House. One prominent absentee, 83-year-old Rep. Frederica Wilson (D-FL), has been recovering from major eye surgery. House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-NY) reportedly urged her to return to Washington promptly due to the fragile numbers. Wilson is expected back next Wednesday. Republican members have also faced similar absences, including Rep. Tom Kean Jr. (R-NJ), who has been out for months due to a medical condition not disclosed to party leadership. The vote comes at a time of mounting pressure on Trump to end U.S. military engagement in Iran, as the conflict has exceeded the 60-day period after which Congress can intervene. The situation underscores the challenges both parties face in main

item.title: A Tribute to Marianna Hill: Iconic Roles and Timeless Portraits from the 1960s and ’70s
item.description: Marianna Hill, an American actress renowned for her exotic beauty and captivating screen presence, emerged as a memorable figure in 1960s and 1970s cinema and television. Hill’s performances ranged from the hauntingly subtle, such as her portrayal of Deanna, Fredo Corleone’s wife in The Godfather Part II, to dynamic guest roles on cult television shows like Star Trek, where she played the telepathic Dr. Helen Noel. Her versatility allowed her to seamlessly transition across genres, from gritty neo-noirs to classic Westerns like High Plains Drifter, earning her a reputation for sophistication and mysterious allure. Beyond her on-screen achievements, Hill’s career highlights the era’s emphasis on character-driven acting and the nuanced performances that defined Hollywood’s Golden Age. This curated photo collection celebrates her radiant charm, elegance, and enduring impact on film and television, reminding audiences of the timeless appeal and artistry of one of Hollywood’s most versatile

item.title: WNBA Board Approves Sale of Connecticut Sun to Tilman Fertitta; Team Will Relocate to Houston After 2026 Season
item.description: The WNBA and NBA boards of governors have unanimously approved the sale of the Connecticut Sun from the Mohegan Tribe to Houston Rockets owner Tilman J. Fertitta. The team will remain in Connecticut for the remainder of the 2026 season, playing two games at PeoplesBank Arena in Hartford and one at TD Garden in Boston, before relocating to Houston ahead of the 2027 campaign. Sun president Jen Rizzotti emphasized that the focus for players, staff, and fans should remain on the final season in Connecticut. Fertitta's acquisition marks the latest instance of NBA owners expanding into WNBA ownership, a trend expected to provide additional resources and infrastructure for the teams. The sale price is reported at $300 million, setting a record for a WNBA franchise. Initial collaboration has begun between Sun and Rockets personnel, including the hiring of former ESPN NBA reporter Kevin Pelton as assistant GM and VP of analytics. Rizzotti noted that staff who wish to move to Houston will be wel

item.title: Early 20th Century French Postcards Depicting Young Women Celebrating April Fool’s Day
item.description: This article explores the history and imagery of early 20th-century French postcards featuring young women celebrating April Fool’s Day, known in French as 'poisson d’avril.' The tradition of April Fool’s Day in France dates back centuries, with one of the earliest references appearing in 1508 by French poet Eloy d’Amerval. The holiday may have originated from differing New Year’s Day celebrations: while most European towns marked New Year on March 25, some regions in France extended the festivities until April 1. Those who celebrated New Year on January 1 reportedly teased others who followed the older calendar, giving rise to April Fool’s Day. By the mid-16th century, January 1 became the common New Year in France and was officially recognized in 1564 through the Edict of Roussillon. The postcards highlighted in the article reflect the humor, fashion, and cultural attitudes of young French women of that era, offering a glimpse into both social norms and festive practices of the time.

item.title: USS Alaska’s Visit to Gibraltar Highlights U.S. Nuclear Deterrence Amid Iran Tensions
item.description: The U.S. Navy’s Ohio-class ballistic missile submarine, the USS Alaska, made a rare public stop in Gibraltar, drawing international attention amid heightened tensions between the United States and Iran. This visit followed President Donald Trump’s rejection of Iran’s latest counterproposal to end hostilities, which included demands such as war reparations, recognition of sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and lifting U.S. sanctions. The Sixth Fleet highlighted the visit as a demonstration of U.S. military capability, flexibility, and commitment to NATO allies. Ohio-class submarines are a critical part of the U.S. nuclear triad, designed to remain hidden at sea and serve as a survivable deterrent against nuclear threats. By publicly revealing the submarine’s location, the Pentagon sent a clear strategic message to Iran, emphasizing the potential consequences of escalating conflict. The USS Alaska bypassed the nearby U.S. naval base in Rota, Spain, instead docking in British Gibralta

item.title: Rep. Jim Jordan challenged on past comments about gas prices amid rising costs
item.description: During a recent segment on CNN's 'The Source,' Representative Jim Jordan (R-OH), a leading candidate for Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives, faced pointed questions from journalist Kaitlan Collins regarding his previous statements on gas prices. Collins highlighted that Jordan had criticized the Biden administration when gas prices reached $3 per gallon during the COVID-19 pandemic. She contrasted that with current prices exceeding $4.50 under the Trump administration amid ongoing geopolitical tensions with Iran. Jordan struggled to respond, initially pivoting to concerns about Iran's nuclear program. Collins then reminded him of his past criticism of Washington 'elites' and President Trump's promise to lower gas prices to under $2 per gallon. When she noted that his dismissive remark, 'That's life,' might be insensitive to families struggling with higher fuel costs, a heated exchange ensued, with Jordan denying his own words and Collins countering immediately. The segment hi

item.title: Florida Gubernatorial Candidate Loses Volunteer Following Offensive Online Comment
item.description: James Fishback, a far-right Florida gubernatorial candidate, lost a key campaign volunteer after allegedly sending her a message containing an obscure internet slur. Gianna Tagliarini, the Hillsborough County chair for Fishback's campaign and a 30-year-old single mother, had been volunteering unpaid when she received a text from Fishback late in the evening. She expressed caution and suggested meeting in a public location. Shortly after, she learned that Fishback had allegedly read the texts aloud to a group of male volunteers, including a minor, and referred to her using the term 'foid,' originating from online forums like 4chan. This incident led Tagliarini to resign from her position. Fishback, a 31-year-old hedge fund investor challenging President Trump's endorsed candidate Rep. Byron Donalds, has a history of controversial statements, including racist remarks and threats of violence. Despite this, he has developed a following among far-right youth and internet influencers. The si

item.title: Ali MacGraw’s Career and Style Evolution in the 1980s
item.description: Ali MacGraw, born April 1, 1939, achieved early fame with her roles in 'Goodbye, Columbus' (1969) and 'Love Story' (1970), earning a Golden Globe and an Academy Award nomination. By 1972, she was recognized as one of the world’s top female film stars, receiving a hands and footprints ceremony at Grauman's Chinese Theatre. In the 1980s, MacGraw transitioned from the 1970s “It Girl” persona to a more mature Hollywood style icon. Her most notable role during this decade was as Lady Ashley Mitchell on the prime-time soap opera 'Dynasty,' where she became a symbol of sophisticated fashion with shoulder pads, sequins, and gold jewelry while maintaining her natural elegance. She also appeared in the WWII miniseries 'The Winds of War' (1983), which was commercially successful but received mixed reviews for her performance, prompting her to step back from major acting roles. Beyond acting, MacGraw influenced 1980s fashion by blending preppy and bohemian styles and focused on wellness, maintaini

item.title: Former Becerra Aide Pleads Guilty in Federal Fraud Case Amid Questions Over Campaign Funds
item.description: Dana Williamson, a longtime political operative with ties to Xavier Becerra, Gov. Gavin Newsom, and former Gov. Jerry Brown, pleaded guilty Thursday in federal court in Sacramento to charges related to a financial scheme. Court documents indicate that Williamson facilitated payments from Becerra's dormant campaign account to Sean McCluskie, a close aide to Becerra, through a complex arrangement. Williamson even contributed $2,500 monthly from her own funds to supplement the $7,500 she received from the account, which raises questions about her motivations and understanding of the scheme. McCluskie had taken a pay cut when Becerra moved to Washington to serve in the Biden administration and asked Williamson to help financially. Williamson’s attorney emphasized that she believed McCluskie had approval from Becerra and that she acted to assist a friend, not for personal gain. Becerra has denied any involvement or wrongdoing, describing the situation as a betrayal by trusted advisors. The

item.title: Fairfax County Prosecutor Denies Leniency Toward Undocumented Immigrants
item.description: Stephen Descano, the attorney for Fairfax County, Virginia, strongly refuted claims made by U.S. House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jim Jordan (R-OH) that his office does not prosecute undocumented immigrants. Jordan highlighted a statement from Descano's campaign website, which mentioned that immigration consequences would be considered in charging and pleading decisions, implying selective leniency. Descano clarified that this language was not representative of official county policy and asserted that his office prosecutes all defendants regardless of immigration status. Jordan referenced a specific case involving an individual named Mr. Morales Ortíz, suggesting he was released due to Descano's supposed policy. Descano firmly rejected this interpretation, stating that immigration status is never a factor in prosecution decisions. The exchange underscores a political debate over immigration enforcement practices and the accuracy of campaign messaging, as Descano accused Jordan of mis

item.title: Debbie Reynolds Performs for U.S. Troops in Seoul During 1955 USO Tour
item.description: On May 22, 1955, iconic movie star Debbie Reynolds performed for thousands of American soldiers stationed at the 8th Army Headquarters in Seoul, South Korea. This performance was part of a week-long USO tour known as the 'Johnny Grant Galaxy' show. Reynolds, who had risen to fame with her breakout role in 'Singin’ in the Rain' three years earlier, traveled with disc jockey Johnny Grant and a troupe of entertainers to deliver morale-boosting performances across South Korea and Japan. The Seoul show was highlighted by a large sign welcoming her, creating a memorable scene for the troops. Photographs from the event depict Reynolds on a simple, elegant makeshift stage, with rows of uniformed soldiers stretching into the distance. At the time, Reynolds was regarded as a quintessential 'girl-next-door' celebrity, admired for her charm and approachability. Veterans recalled her dazzling presence and the effort she made to bring entertainment to a region still recovering from the Korean War. J

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Portraits of Jane Birkin During the Filming of La Piscine
item.description: In 1969, Jane Birkin brought a distinctive charm to the set of the psychological thriller 'La Piscine,' portraying the character Penelope with a blend of youthful curiosity and understated allure. Set against the sunny backdrop of St. Tropez, Birkin's natural elegance and bohemian style captured the essence of late 1960s French Riviera fashion. Her iconic gamine bob, wide-eyed expressions, and minimalist wardrobe of knitwear and crochet highlighted her unique aesthetic, making her a lasting symbol of French New Wave cinema. Sharing the screen with renowned actors Alain Delon and Romy Schneider, Birkin's presence radiated a relaxed, magnetic energy, exemplifying a liberated and modern femininity that resonated with audiences at the time. These rare behind-the-scenes photographs not only showcase her fashion and style but also offer insight into her effortless charisma and the atmosphere of one of the most stylish film sets of the 1960s. This photo collection serves as a visual testament

item.title: Federal Judge Blocks Key Sections of Texas Immigration Law Before Enforcement
item.description: A Texas law, Senate Bill 4, which would have allowed state and local police to arrest individuals suspected of crossing the southern border illegally, has been halted by a federal judge just a day before it was set to take effect. Passed in 2023, SB 4 designates illegal border crossings as a state crime and requires magistrate judges to order deportation to Mexico for those convicted or in lieu of prosecution. Civil rights groups challenged the law, arguing that it infringes on federal immigration authority and fails to protect individuals with legal or pending immigration status. U.S. District Judge David Alan Ezra granted a preliminary injunction, citing the constitutional conflict, emphasizing that each state cannot create its own immigration policy. Advocacy groups including the ACLU highlighted concerns about racial profiling and the overreach of state authority. Previously, the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals dismissed a similar case, but on procedural grounds rather than evalu

item.title: Georgia Republican Governor Candidate’s Spouse Donates to Democratic Senator
item.description: Rick Jackson is running to be the Republican nominee for governor in Georgia, yet a surprising detail has emerged regarding his family. According to Federal Election Commission filings, his wife, Melody Jackson, made a $1,000 contribution in February 2025 to Democratic Senator Jon Ossoff, a key figure Republicans aim to contest. The donation lists her residence in Cumming, Georgia — the same area as the Jackson family estate — and identifies her occupation as a Georgia Tech professor, consistent with public records. Jackson, a billionaire CEO of Jackson Healthcare, has faced scrutiny for contradictory stances on policy issues. Notably, his company publicly criticized President Trump’s “Big Beautiful Bill,” citing concerns about Medicaid and ACA program cuts potentially affecting hospitals. Despite having endorsements from prominent figures like Newt Gingrich, Fran Tarkenton, and Georgia House Speaker Pro Tempore Jan Jones, Jackson is competing in a crowded Republican primary that inclu

item.title: Profile of U.S. Farmers Market Shoppers: Trends and Motivations
item.description: A study of U.S. consumers reveals that people who shop at the more than 8,700 farmers markets across the country generally fall into six distinct groups. Three groups—the highly engaged, health-focused, and emerging interest shoppers—show strong interest in farmers markets, primarily motivated by a desire for fresh, healthy food, supporting local farmers, and enjoying the shopping experience. Highly engaged shoppers (18%) are diverse, enjoy cooking, and have lower household incomes, while health-focused shoppers (18%) prioritize healthy food but cook less and tend to be older and predominantly white. Emerging interest shoppers (19%) enjoy the social and fun aspects of farmers markets and are the youngest group. The remaining groups—convenience (16%), practical (17%), and uninvolved (12%) shoppers—are less interested, often buying convenience foods or rarely visiting markets. Forgetting to go was cited as the main reason people skipped trips across all groups. This information could hel

item.title: Betty Hutton’s Early Modeling Work for Bonwit Teller in 1940
item.description: In December 1940, a young Betty Hutton, aged 19, appeared in LOOK Magazine modeling an ice skating costume for the luxury Fifth Avenue department store, Bonwit Teller. At the time, Hutton was gaining fame as a musical comedy performer on Broadway in Ethel Merman’s Panama Hattie, but had not yet become the Hollywood star celebrated for Annie Get Your Gun. The costume featured a military-inspired, heavily structured jacket with detailed braiding and a flared skirt designed for skating, reflecting the era’s trend of merging functional sportswear with high-fashion elegance. Hutton’s energetic personality brought a sense of movement and life to the photos, contrasting with the stiff poses common in 1940s fashion spreads. The shoot exemplified the popularity of figure skating-inspired fashion, driven in part by Sonja Henie’s influence, and represented Bonwit Teller’s effort to appeal to a younger, more vibrant audience. These modeling stints contributed to Hutton’s transition from band singe

item.title: The Economic and Environmental Value of Bats in U.S. Agriculture
item.description: Bats play a crucial role in the U.S. economy and ecosystem by eating insects, pollinating plants, and providing natural fertilizer through their guano. Yet, bat populations are rapidly declining due to white-nose syndrome, a fungal disease that has spread across 47 states and threatens several species, including endangered ones. This decline has direct economic consequences: bats help control pests like cucumber beetles, which would otherwise devastate crops such as corn. A single colony of 150 big brown bats can prevent the production of millions of harmful rootworms, protecting thousands of acres of farmland. The loss of bats leads to increased pesticide use, reduced crop yields, and higher costs for farmers. Additionally, local governments experience lower property tax revenue when agricultural profitability declines, costing rural counties millions annually. Municipal bond markets are also affected, as reduced county revenue increases perceived investment risk, raising borrowing co

item.title: California Lawmakers Raise Concerns About Federal Support and Immigration Issues Ahead of 2028 Olympics
item.description:
As Los Angeles prepares to host the 2028 Olympics, state lawmakers are expressing concerns about potential complications stemming from tensions with the Trump administration. Key issues include the security and transportation funding necessary for the Games, which rely on approximately $1 billion in federal support. State Senator Susan Rubio highlighted the risk that federal funding might not be guaranteed due to strained relations between California and the federal government. Despite assurances from LA28 Organizing Committee officials about a cooperative relationship with the Trump administration, Rubio emphasized that state resources are limited and expressed concern about the state being forced to cover funding gaps.
Lawmakers are also focused on protecting immigrant communities during the Olympics, anticipating increased Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) activity. Legislation is being drafted to safeguard visitors and residents from potential federal enforcement actions.

item.title: The Art and Legacy of Bernard Blossac in 20th Century Fashion Illustration
item.description: Bernard Blossac (1917–2004) was a prominent French fashion illustrator known for his sophisticated and graceful depictions of haute couture. His work, primarily executed in ink and watercolor, showcased the elegance and refinement of postwar Paris fashion, capturing the movement and texture of fabrics with remarkable precision. Blossac collaborated with leading fashion publications such as Vogue, Harper’s Bazaar, and Femina, and worked with prestigious designers including Dior, Balenciaga, Givenchy, Chanel, Jacques Fath, and Marcel Rochas. His illustrations highlighted the intricate details of both daywear and evening gowns, from flowing dresses to tailored suits, often emphasizing long, elegant lines and a keen sense of feminine chic. Blossac’s drawings documented iconic fashion moments of the 1940s and 1950s, including Dior’s “New Look” and Balenciaga’s grand ball gowns, serving as a visual record of an era defined by glamour and sophistication. Today, his work remains a significant

item.title: Apple Launches iOS 26.5 with Encrypted RCS Messaging and New Features
item.description: Apple has officially released iOS 26.5, available for iPhone 11 models and newer, including the latest iPhone 17 series. Users can update via Settings > General > Software Update. The long-anticipated new version of Siri is not included in this release and is now expected to debut with iOS 27 at WWDC on June 8. A major highlight of iOS 26.5 is the introduction of end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging, which will secure communications between iPhones and Android devices. Currently in beta, this feature requires carrier support and is gradually being rolled out. Encrypted conversations will be indicated by a new lock icon within RCS chats, with encryption enabled automatically over time. The update also brings security fixes for over 50 vulnerabilities, some critical, including kernel and WebKit issues, as well as sandbox escape threats. Additional features include a new 'Pride Luminance' wallpaper that dynamically reflects colors and an updated Maps app offering 'Suggested Places' based on

item.title: Starship Block 3 Prepares for 12th Test Flight with Major Upgrades
item.description: After a seven-month hiatus, SpaceX's Starship system is scheduled for its 12th test mission (IFT-12) on May 19, 2026, marking a critical step in the development of the world's most powerful rocket. This flight introduces the Starship Block 3 (v3) along with a completely new launch pad, OLP-2. Block 3 features significant upgrades to both the Super Heavy booster and the Starship second stage, including more powerful Raptor 3 engines, redesigned fuel lines, improved aerodynamic control fins, and enhanced orbital maneuvering engines. The Super Heavy Block 3 now carries 3,650 tons of propellant and delivers 8,250 tons of thrust at liftoff. The new launch pad has advanced safety features like a water-cooled exhaust trench and more robust anchoring systems, reducing fueling time from 49 to 38 minutes. The mission will deploy 22 Starlink satellite mock-ups and test critical thermal protection and reentry systems, while the booster and Starship stages will perform controlled water landings in

item.title: Keith Richards’ Humorous T-Shirt During the Rolling Stones’ 1975 American Tour
item.description: During the Rolling Stones’ 1975 Tour of the Americas, guitarist Keith Richards wore a memorable white t-shirt with the bold statement, “Who the F*ck is Mick Jagger?” This shirt, often misunderstood as a personal jab at the band’s frontman, was actually a playful gift from Atlantic Records. It was intended as a humorous commentary on the intense public and media focus on Mick Jagger’s persona at the time. As Jagger increasingly embodied the jet-setting, high-society image of the band, Richards remained the rebellious, gritty rock icon. The shirt became a symbolic representation of their contrasting personalities and roles within the band. Richards wore it both backstage and during rehearsals, and it stood out against his usual dark, layered stage attire. The 1975 tour itself was chaotic and energetic, capturing the essence of the Rolling Stones at their creative peak. Richards’ pirate-like aesthetic, including shaggy hair, kohl-rimmed eyes, and scarves, made the t-shirt an iconic visual

item.title: Early Signs of El Niño in 2026: Uncertain Forecasts and Preparation Guidance
item.description: In 2026, scientists are observing signals that suggest a potential strong El Niño, a climate pattern capable of causing extreme weather globally, including heavy rainfall, droughts, and heatwaves. Subsurface ocean temperatures in the eastern equatorial Pacific have warmed significantly, partially triggered by unusual wind patterns in April that pushed warm water eastward. While these signals indicate the possibility of a major El Niño event, forecasting remains uncertain, especially in spring, because the ocean-atmosphere feedback loop that sustains El Niño is not guaranteed to form. Past forecasts in 2014 and 2017 predicted strong events that ultimately did not develop fully due to atmospheric conditions. Current models for 2026-27 show a wide range of potential outcomes, from weak to strong El Niño conditions. The intensity of the event matters greatly, as strong El Niño events can disrupt global weather patterns, affecting monsoons in India, hurricane activity in the Atlantic, and r

item.title: Daily Life in Kyoto in the Early 1900s Through Vintage Photographs
item.description: This article presents a collection of vintage photographs showcasing everyday life in Kyoto, Japan, during the early 20th century. The images capture the delicate balance between tradition and the beginnings of modernization. Residents lived in machiya, traditional wooden townhouses, where family life intertwined with small-scale craftsmanship. The streets of Kyoto were filled with Geiko and Maiko navigating the alleys of Gion, monks in saffron robes passing temple gates, and ordinary citizens engaging in daily routines. At the same time, subtle signs of modernization appeared, such as electric streetcars and Western-style school uniforms, reflecting a society gradually adapting to new influences while retaining its historical culture. Photographs include scenes from festivals, temple grounds, streets, and countryside excursions, offering a visual record of Kyoto’s unique atmosphere during this era. The article emphasizes the simplicity and beauty of daily life, highlighting the contra

item.title: CNN commentator criticizes inclusion of Brett Ratner in Trump's official China delegation
item.description: During President Donald Trump's official trip to China, filmmaker Brett Ratner accompanied the U.S. delegation, traveling on Air Force One at taxpayer expense. CNN commentator Ana Navarro strongly criticized this decision, citing Ratner's past sexual harassment allegations and his previous ostracization from Hollywood in 2017. Navarro highlighted that Ratner’s name appears in the Epstein files, raising further ethical concerns. She expressed that Ratner’s involvement in the trip suggests he might have been politically rehabilitated by Trump, calling the situation 'appalling.' Navarro urged viewers to consider the experiences of women who publicly accused Ratner of harassment and assault. The trip itself focused on trade, regional security, and strengthening bilateral relations between the United States and China, with President Trump and U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth participating in high-level meetings with President Xi Jinping. Navarro's criticism brings attention to the eth

item.title: Apple rolls out iOS 26.5 with encrypted RCS messaging and security fixes
item.description:
Apple has released iOS 26.5 for supported iPhones, bringing one of the most anticipated messaging upgrades to the platform. The standout addition is end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging, currently rolling out in beta for users with supported carriers. This feature is important because it improves privacy and security in chats between iPhone and Android users, bringing cross-platform messaging closer to the protection already available in iMessage. The update also includes more than 50 security fixes, with several affecting core parts of iOS and WebKit, making it an important install even for users who are not interested in the new features.
The release does not include the much-talked-about new Siri, which is still expected later, likely with iOS 27. Apple also added a Pride Luminance wallpaper and a Maps feature called Suggested Places for users in the United States. In the European Union, the update introduces changes tied to the Digital Markets Act, including broader access for third

item.title: House Vote on Iran War Resolution Ends in Tie, Highlighting GOP Division
item.description: On May 14, 2026, the U.S. House of Representatives held a critical vote on a war powers resolution aimed at requiring President Donald Trump to obtain congressional approval before continuing military operations in Iran. The measure, sponsored by Rep. Josh Gottheimer (D-N.J.), was ultimately tied at 212-212, resulting in a failed vote. Notably, three House Republicans—Reps. Thomas Massie (Ky.), Brian Fitzpatrick (Pa.), and Tom Barrett (Mich.)—joined Democrats in support of the resolution. This represents a shift for Fitzpatrick and Barrett, who had previously opposed similar measures, and signals growing unease within the GOP regarding Trump's ongoing military actions without congressional authorization. Democrats have now brought three such resolutions to the floor this year, and they plan to continue doing so. Public opposition to Trump's handling of the conflict in Iran is rising, putting additional pressure on Republican lawmakers facing tight reelection races, including Barrett. M

item.title: North Carolina Senate candidate Michael Whatley criticized for selling China-made campaign merchandise
item.description: Michael Whatley, a Senate candidate in North Carolina aligned with the 'America First' movement, has come under scrutiny for distributing campaign merchandise manufactured in China. Supporters of Whatley received drink koozies that were imported from China, tied to a campaign expense exceeding $1,600 as reported in a recent Federal Elections Commission filing. This has raised concerns because Whatley has publicly criticized China for harming American workers by flooding the market with low-cost goods and has supported tariffs on Chinese imports during the Trump administration. The controversy mirrors similar actions by former President Donald Trump, whose private businesses and campaigns have also sold merchandise produced in countries such as China, Haiti, and Nicaragua. Despite Whatley's criticism of foreign manufacturing, Trump has described him as a patriot advocating for 'Made in the U.S.A.' products. The incident highlights a potential inconsistency between political rhetoric and

item.title: Marlon Brando’s Formative Years and Rise in 1940s Theater
item.description: Before achieving legendary status as the star of 'The Godfather,' Marlon Brando transformed American acting during the 1940s. Arriving in New York as a teenager in 1943, Brando studied at the Dramatic Workshop at the New School under the mentorship of Stella Adler, who encouraged him to discover the psychological truth of his characters rather than merely performing emotions. His Broadway debut in 'I Remember Mama' in 1944 showcased a magnetic realism that set him apart from other actors. Brando’s iconic role as Stanley Kowalski in Tennessee Williams’ 'A Streetcar Named Desire,' directed by Elia Kazan, redefined stage performance. With his animalistic yet vulnerable portrayal and naturalistic delivery, he stunned audiences and influenced theater and film standards. The 1940s concluded with Brando preparing for his film debut in 'The Men,' immersing himself in a veterans’ hospital to authentically portray a paraplegic soldier. By 1949, Brando was America’s most discussed young actor, po

item.title: Sean Duffy's Family Road Trip Reality Show Funded by Companies Under His Oversight
item.description: Sean Duffy, President-elect Donald Trump's nominee for U.S. Transportation Secretary, is facing scrutiny over a reality TV project. Duffy filmed a family road trip series called 'The Great American Roadtrip' with his wife and nine children. The trip, lasting seven months, was promoted as a wholesome family experience. However, investigative reports revealed that the production was funded by a nonprofit organization supported by major transportation industry companies, including Boeing, Toyota, Shell, Royal Caribbean, United Airlines, Chase Travel, and the U.S. Travel Association. Sponsorship packages for the show ranged from $100,000 to $1 million. As Transportation Secretary, Duffy oversees regulations affecting aviation, automotive safety, gas pipelines, and hazardous materials transportation—areas directly relevant to the sponsoring companies. Despite being a multimillionaire with a taxpayer-funded salary, Duffy did not personally finance the trip or the reality show. Critics sugges

item.title: Wisconsin Democrats Consider Redistricting to Gain House Seats
item.description: Wisconsin Democrats are exploring an aggressive mid-decade redistricting strategy if they secure unified control of the state government in the upcoming election. This plan could potentially result in Democrats gaining four additional seats in the U.S. House of Representatives. The strategy comes in response to a series of Republican-led redistricting efforts in the South, which targeted Black-majority districts following Supreme Court decisions that made it harder to challenge such maps under the Voting Rights Act. Democrats see Wisconsin, a consistent swing state, as a prime opportunity to reverse the current 6-to-2 Republican advantage in the state’s congressional delegation. State Democratic Party Chairman Devin Remiker expressed optimism about achieving a 6-to-2 Democratic map, emphasizing ambitious goals. While Democrats had hoped that the state’s liberal Supreme Court would invalidate the existing GOP gerrymander, litigation timelines likely prevent a court-ordered redraw before

item.title: Behind the Scenes of the 1971 Cult Classic 'Red Sun'
item.description: The 1971 film 'Red Sun,' also known as 'Soleil Rouge,' directed by Terence Young, is a landmark in the Spaghetti Western genre, combining American cowboy motifs with Japanese samurai traditions. The story is set in Arizona around 1870 and follows a train robbery led by outlaws Link Stuart (Charles Bronson) and Gauche (Alain Delon). The train carries a priceless ceremonial sword from the Japanese Emperor to the U.S. President. Gauche steals the sword and betrays Link, leaving him for dead. The Japanese ambassador assigns Kuroda Jubei (Toshirō Mifune) to retrieve the sword within seven days, under threat of ritual suicide if he fails. Forced into a reluctant alliance, Link and Kuroda navigate a world of gunfights and sword duels, highlighting the cultural contrasts between East and West. Although filmed in Spain as a Franco-Italian-Spanish co-production, the movie achieved its greatest success in Japan and Europe, while receiving mixed reviews in the United States. 'Red Sun' is considere

item.title: Study Finds Minimal Link Between Prenatal Antidepressant Use and ADHD or Autism
item.description: A large review and meta-analysis of 37 studies involving over 600,000 pregnant women and nearly 25 million pregnancies found that antidepressant use before or during pregnancy has little to no significant association with ADHD or autism in children once other factors are considered. Initial results suggested higher risks, but after adjusting for confounding factors such as genetic influences and underlying maternal conditions, most associations weakened substantially. The study, published in The Lancet Psychiatry, also highlighted the importance of paternal mental health and antidepressant use in neurodevelopmental outcomes. The research provides reassurance that continuing necessary antidepressant treatment during pregnancy is generally safe, aligning with current clinical guidelines. Older tricyclic antidepressants showed a potential link to ADHD, but SSRIs, the most commonly prescribed antidepressants, did not show significant risk. Experts emphasized that untreated maternal depress

item.title: Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao Defends Controversial Debate Comments in Congressional Hearing
item.description: Acting Secretary of the Navy Hung Cao stood by remarks he made during a 2024 Senate debate while testifying before the House Armed Services Committee on May 14, 2026. Cao, who previously ran unsuccessfully for the U.S. Senate in Virginia, was questioned by Rep. Chrissy Houlahan about a comment in which he suggested the military needs 'alpha males and alpha females who are going to rip out their guts, eat them, and ask for seconds' as an example of the type of people who will win wars. Cao confirmed he made the statement and did not retract it. The original comment was made during a televised debate against Democratic Senator Tim Kaine at Norfolk State University, in response to questions about the military's diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives. Cao, a retired Navy captain with experience in special operations in Iraq, Afghanistan, and Somalia, was appointed Acting Secretary of the Navy in April after the abrupt removal of his predecessor by former President Trump and Defense S

item.title: Supreme Court allows continued telehealth access to abortion pill amid legal dispute
item.description: On May 14, 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court temporarily blocked a lower court ruling that would have forced women to obtain the abortion pill Mifepristone through in-person visits. This decision allows women to continue accessing the medication via telehealth while legal proceedings continue. The ruling preserves the existing process as officials in Louisiana pursue restrictions in lower courts. The Fifth Circuit had issued a decision on May 1 requiring in-person visits, but the Supreme Court maintained a stay on this requirement. Justices Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito dissented, though the court did not provide an explanation or disclose the vote count. The focus now returns to the New Orleans-based appeals court, which will review the merits of Louisiana's challenge. The case highlights ongoing national debates over abortion access, telehealth services, and state-level regulatory authority. This temporary ruling ensures that women in the U.S. can maintain access to Mifepristone withou

item.title: Steyer Campaign Staffer Connected to Leaked Video of Katie Porter
item.description: A memo and reporting suggest that a current Tom Steyer campaign spokesperson was involved in filming and editing a 2021 U.S. Department of Energy Zoom call in which former Rep. Katie Porter was seen berating a staffer. The video, later leaked to Politico, negatively affected Porter's gubernatorial campaign. Porter publicly accused the Steyer campaign of leaking the clip during a CNN interview, asserting that the video’s trail could be traced to Steyer’s staff. Steyer's campaign denied any direct involvement by the candidate. The briefing memo indicates that Kevin Liao, a Steyer campaign spokesman and former press secretary for Energy Secretary Jennifer Granholm, was expected to participate in the recording and assist in editing it for social media. The original Zoom call focused on promoting electric vehicles, with Granholm and Porter participating. Although Porter apologized to the staffer involved, the video reinforced criticisms about her temperament, affecting voter perceptions. Pr

item.title: Lucile Manguin: Iconic 1950s Fashion and Artistic Legacy
item.description: Lucile Manguin, born in 1899, was a pioneering French fashion designer and gallerist whose influence on mid-20th-century Parisian fashion remains notable. Operating her high-fashion house from 1928 to 1960, Manguin became renowned for her sophisticated evening gowns, innovative silhouettes, and distinctive collection presentations, often showcased by candlelight. As the daughter and muse of Fauve painter Henri Manguin, she frequently appeared in his artwork, linking her life and work to both the fashion and art worlds. Beyond her designs, Manguin contributed to the arts through her gallery, creating a bridge between textile design and fine art. The article highlights a curated selection of her 1950s creations, featuring elegant draping, pleated skirts, elaborate embroidery, and luxurious fabrics such as silk, satin, and lace. Notable models such as Gigi Terwalgne, Bettina Graziani, and Sophie Malgat are pictured wearing Manguin’s evening gowns, tailored suits, and statement coats, phot

item.title: Exploring Life and Memory in Thokoza Through Photography
item.description: Sibusiso Bheka, a photographer born in 1997 in Thokoza, a township south of Johannesburg, captures the nuanced realities of South Africa's post-apartheid 'born-free' generation. His work explores the intersection of memory, history, and contemporary life in a place marked by political and social upheaval. Through projects like 'At Night They Walk With Me' and 'Stop Nonsense,' Bheka uses night photography and existing light to create dreamlike, ghostly images that reflect both the freedoms and lingering dangers of township life. His images often feature walls or lines that symbolize social and historical divisions, while their distinctive orange hue references the artificial lighting imposed during apartheid. Bheka’s latest project, 'SEENS,' shifts to daylight photography, focusing on childhood memories of exploring his neighborhood and observing neighbors, blending reality and fiction to reveal unexpected truths about everyday life. Across his work, Bheka uses storytelling to highlight

item.title: Paramount explores podcast deal with Katie Miller amid criticism
item.description: Paramount, a major media company with ties to former President Donald Trump, is reportedly in discussions to acquire or distribute 'The Katie Miller Podcast,' hosted by Katie Miller, the wife of White House Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller. Despite having a modest following of just over 57,000 on YouTube and no episodes surpassing 1 million views, the podcast has featured prominent conservative figures such as Sean Hannity, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., and Elon Musk. The potential deal comes as Paramount seeks to expand its podcast offerings and diversify its voice, according to Axios. However, the news has drawn widespread mockery and criticism from political analysts and commentators on social media, with some calling it a form of favoritism toward the family of a powerful Trump aide, and others questioning the podcast's popularity and merit. The Lincoln Project described the situation as an ongoing 'grift,' while various writers highlighted the perceived business and ethical risks of

item.title: Former FBI Official Warns That Virus Response and CDC Staffing Cuts Pose Major Health Risks
item.description: A former high-ranking FBI official, Frank Figliuzzi, has raised concerns about significant vulnerabilities in the U.S. public health system due to actions taken during the Trump administration. According to Figliuzzi, specialized teams responsible for monitoring and responding to new strains of viruses, including hantavirus, have been dismantled. Hantavirus, a potentially fatal virus with no known cure, has recently caused deaths among passengers on an infected cruise ship. Figliuzzi also noted that units handling cruise line health inspections were eliminated and thousands of epidemiologists who could have supported pandemic preparedness are no longer employed. He warned that these reductions have left critical gaps in the Centers for Disease Control and related Health and Human Services agencies, with around 80 percent of senior roles vacant. The former FBI official emphasized that these cuts create a serious national security risk, not only for naturally occurring pandemics but also

item.title: The Artistic Legacy of Petrus van Schendel: Master of Candlelit Scenes
item.description: Petrus van Schendel (1806–1870) was a prominent Dutch-Belgian painter of the Romantic era, celebrated for his exceptional skill in depicting night scenes illuminated by candles and moonlight. Often called “Monsieur Chandelle” (Mr. Candlelight), he brought a unique warmth and intimacy to everyday market scenes and nocturnal cityscapes, transforming ordinary life into dramatic works of art. His paintings exemplify the chiaroscuro technique, creating a compelling contrast between light and shadow, and his attention to detail reflected both artistic sensibility and scientific knowledge of optics and perspective. Van Schendel's works often showcase busy market stalls, moonlit canals, and quiet domestic interiors, portraying a 19th-century world where light defined mood and narrative. Beyond his artistic contributions, he was also a mechanical engineer, and his technical background enhanced his mastery of light effects. Collections of his works reveal a fascination with everyday life, illumi

item.title: Evidence of Preserved Collagen Found in 66-Million-Year-Old Edmontosaurus Fossils
item.description: A groundbreaking study from the University of Liverpool has revealed that dinosaur fossils may still contain traces of original proteins, challenging the long-held assumption that fossilization completely destroys organic material. Researchers examined a 22-kilogram Edmontosaurus sacrum fossil from South Dakota's Hell Creek Formation and discovered remnants of collagen, a key structural protein in bone, using advanced techniques like mass spectrometry and protein sequencing. Hydroxyproline, an amino acid associated with collagen, was also identified, strengthening the evidence that these molecules are genuinely endogenous and not contamination. This finding supports previous controversial reports of preserved soft tissues in dinosaur fossils, including earlier discoveries in Tyrannosaurus rex. The study's results suggest that some fossils act as molecular time capsules, potentially providing insights into dinosaur physiology, growth, aging, disease, and evolutionary relationships. The

item.title: R&B Legend Clarence Carter Passes Away at 90
item.description: Clarence Carter, the renowned blues and soul singer-songwriter known for his distinctive raspy voice, has died at the age of 90. Born blind in Montgomery, Alabama, Carter taught himself guitar and pursued music studies at Alabama State College. He achieved his biggest commercial success in 1970 with 'Patches,' a poignant story about a young man taking responsibility for his family’s farm after his father's death. While Carter is celebrated for such sentimental hits, he was equally famous for his more risqué songs, including 'Strokin’,' 'Slip Away,' 'Back Door Santa,' and 'Making Love on the Dark End of the Street,' which became club favorites despite being too explicit for mainstream radio. Carter’s work included collaborations with notable musicians like Duane Allman and recordings at Muscle Shoals’ Fame Studios, a legendary hub for soul music. His influence extended to other artists; for example, his song 'Tell Daddy' inspired Etta James’ hit 'Tell Mama.' Carter was briefly married t

item.title: Trump Administration Reviews Green Cards of Certain Legal Permanent Residents
item.description: The Trump administration has initiated a new effort targeting legal permanent residents in the United States, according to a report by The New York Times. The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has established a dedicated unit tasked with reviewing thousands of green card holders across the country. The goal is to identify individuals who may have obtained residency through fraud or who may pose a security threat. While over 50 green card holders are reportedly at risk of deportation, the DHS unit has reviewed nearly 3,000 cases so far, with only around 2% deemed potentially deportable. More than 500 green cards are currently under review. This initiative signals an expansion of the immigration crackdown beyond undocumented immigrants to include those who have legal permanent status. Immigration and advocacy groups have expressed concern about the program, noting the potential for broad implications on lawful residents. The creation of this unit highlights how aggressively the admin

item.title: House Ethics Committee investigates GOP Congressman for alleged sexual harassment
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has officially launched an investigation into Republican Congressman Chuck Edwards of North Carolina following allegations of sexual harassment. Reports indicate that Edwards, who has been married since 1980, allegedly gave personal gifts and vacations to two young female staffers in their 20s. Additionally, he faces accusations of a long-term affair with a former deputy chief of staff. Witnesses have also noted uncomfortable behavior during office events, including Edwards becoming tearful while reciting a poem for a departing staffer. This investigation adds to a series of ongoing ethics probes in Congress, including recent resignations of Representatives Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales due to sexual misconduct allegations. Edwards has denied any wrongdoing, asserting that a thorough Ethics Committee investigation will reveal the truth behind the rumors and gossip. He currently represents much of western North Carolina and is preparing for a competitive mid

item.title: Wuffles Joint Chews Improve Mobility and Comfort in Senior Dogs
item.description: Senior dogs often face challenges with mobility, stiffness, and joint pain, making daily activities and playtime difficult. Wuffles hip and joint chews, currently discounted during Amazon Pet Days, have received multiple positive reviews from dog owners for effectively supporting joint health and overall mobility. The chews contain vet-approved ingredients designed to maintain cartilage, promote joint integrity, and support long-term joint health. Owners report significant improvements within weeks to months, including easier walking, running, and jumping, even in dogs over 10 years old. One owner shared that their 11-year-old dog regained enough agility to jump a five-foot fence after six months of daily chews, while others observed relief from lipomas and chronic limping. The product comes in single, two-pack, and three-pack options, with 60 chews per jar, covering about two months depending on the dog's size. Daily use, often starting with two chews in the morning and evening, then

item.title: Niklaus Stoecklin and His Legacy in Swiss Poster Art
item.description: Niklaus Stoecklin (1896–1982) was a pivotal figure in 20th-century Swiss art, renowned for his contributions to the New Objectivity (Neue Sachlichkeit) movement and modern poster design. His work is distinguished by its meticulous precision, transforming ordinary objects such as industrial machines or everyday consumer products into striking symbols of clarity and beauty. Stoecklin successfully merged fine art with commercial graphic design, producing iconic posters for brands including Maggi and Binaca. These works exemplify hyper-realism and the Sachplakat (Object Poster) style, which emphasizes simplicity and essence over decorative detail. Across his career, Stoecklin captured both the silent elegance of mundane objects and the subtle tension of modern life, often imbuing his pieces with a magic realist quality. The article provides a curated list of his most celebrated works from the 1920s to the 1960s, demonstrating the artist's enduring influence on Swiss graphic design and visu

item.title: Doris Day’s Lifelong Dedication to Animal Welfare
item.description: Doris Day, renowned actress and singer, was equally famous for her deep love and dedication to animals. Her passion began after the loss of her childhood dog, Tiny, which inspired her lifelong mission to protect and care for animals. She was rarely seen without a canine companion and often rescued strays, personally ensuring that prospective adopters provided suitable homes. Her Carmel-by-the-Sea residence became a sanctuary for dozens of rescued dogs and cats, earning her the affectionate nickname 'Dog Lady' from locals. Day's activism extended to her professional life; on the set of 'The Man Who Knew Too Much' (1956), she demanded proper care for emaciated animals in Morocco before continuing work. She also co-owned a hotel in Carmel, transforming it into one of the first truly pet-friendly boutique hotels, encouraging guests to bring their pets. Beyond her acting career, she founded the Doris Day Animal Foundation (DDAF) and the Doris Day Animal League, championing causes like spay/

item.title: Trump Administration Subpoenas Wall Street Journal and Other Outlets Seeking Sources on Iran War Reporting
item.description: The US Justice Department, under President Donald Trump's administration, has issued grand jury subpoenas to The Wall Street Journal and other major news organizations regarding their coverage of the ongoing Iran war. The subpoenas, dated March 4, target records of journalists who reported on sensitive national security matters, including Pentagon warnings about the risks of an extended military campaign against Iran. This action was reportedly pushed by Trump himself, who has repeatedly expressed frustration with media coverage he views as damaging to national security. According to reports, Trump handed Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche a stack of articles marked with a 'treason' note. The specific Wall Street Journal article in question, published on February 23, detailed concerns from Joint Chiefs of Staff Chairman Gen. Dan Caine and other officials about potential high costs and challenges of the operation. Trump launched the military action five days later on February 28. Dow

item.title: China expresses concern about UK government's plan to nationalize British Steel
item.description: The Chinese government has voiced concerns over the United Kingdom's proposed plan to nationalize British Steel, signaling potential international economic implications. Officials in China warned that such a move could affect foreign investment in the UK and disrupt trade relations between the two countries. British Steel, a major player in the steel industry, has faced financial difficulties, prompting the UK government to consider taking control to secure jobs and stabilize production. Experts note that nationalization could lead to broader geopolitical tensions, especially given China’s stake in global steel markets and trade partnerships with Europe. This development is seen as part of a larger trend of governments intervening in strategic industries to protect domestic economic interests amid global market uncertainties. The UK government argues that the move is necessary to safeguard employment and maintain industrial capacity, while critics warn about the long-term risks to inve

item.title: Rubio emphasizes U.S. need to innovate to counter China's technology advancement
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio highlighted the urgency for the United States to maintain a technological and innovative edge over China, citing extensive intellectual property theft as a key factor in Beijing’s rapid progress. In an interview on 'Hannity,' Rubio explained that much of China’s advancements in commercial, industrial, and technological sectors are based on either stolen American technology or reverse engineering. He stressed that enforcement measures alone will not suffice and emphasized that the U.S. must continue to invest in cutting-edge technologies to remain globally competitive. Rubio pointed out that China has a clear plan to become the world's most powerful country, and the United States needs to ensure that China’s rise does not come at the expense of American leadership. His comments come ahead of a high-level summit in China involving President Donald Trump, key U.S. officials, and business leaders, where discussions on trade, artificial intelligence, and

item.title: Alameda County settles wrongful death lawsuit for $36 million after deputy’s crimes
item.description: Alameda County has agreed to pay $36 million to the family of Benison and Maria Tran after a wrongful death lawsuit stemming from the September 2022 killings by off-duty Deputy Devin Williams. The incident involved Williams using his department-issued firearm to kill the couple in their Dublin home, witnessed by their 14-year-old son and other family members. Court records revealed serious lapses by the Alameda County Sheriff’s Department, including turning off body cameras, falsifying a 911 report, and advising Maria Tran that she could not pursue a restraining order against Williams. Williams had previously failed a pre-employment psychological evaluation, yet he was still hired, prompting broader scrutiny of the department. In October 2024, Williams was convicted of two counts of first-degree murder and sentenced to 50 years to life in prison. The settlement aims to provide some financial relief to the victims’ surviving family, while highlighting failures in deputy hiring and respo

item.title: Eddie Murphy’s Early Years on Saturday Night Live and His Lasting Impact
item.description: Eddie Murphy joined Saturday Night Live at just 19 years old, during a period when the show was struggling after the departure of its original cast and creator, Lorne Michaels, in 1980. His arrival revitalized the show, and he quickly became its breakout star. Murphy’s charisma and unforgettable characters kept audiences engaged, making him the first cast member whose popularity the show largely centered around. In 1982, he even hosted the show while still a regular cast member due to a co-star’s absence. During his tenure from 1980 to 1984, Murphy created some of SNL’s most iconic recurring characters before leaving to pursue a highly successful film career with hits like Beverly Hills Cop and Coming to America. Despite a long absence from the show due to a joke made by David Spade in the 1990s, Murphy eventually returned for the 40th Anniversary Special in 2015 and officially hosted again in December 2019, earning his first Emmy for Outstanding Guest Actor. His early contributions to

item.title: Francesca Annis: A Retrospective of Her Iconic Career and 1960s–70s Portraits
item.description: Francesca Annis stands as a timeless figure in British theatre and cinema, celebrated for a career spanning more than six decades. She first captured international attention as Lady Macbeth in Roman Polanski’s 1971 adaptation of Macbeth, quickly establishing her reputation as a performer capable of complex, nuanced roles. Known for her aristocratic poise and expressive, soulful gaze, Annis became a sought-after actress for period dramas, earning acclaim for her award-winning portrayal of Lillie Langtry in Edward the Seventh. Beyond classical roles, she demonstrated remarkable versatility, moving seamlessly from epic films like David Lynch’s Dune to contemporary series including Cranford and Home Fires. Annis is distinguished by a unique blend of vulnerability and determination—a “quiet fire” that resonates through her performances. Her striking beauty, intelligence, and enduring elegance have made her a lasting icon of cinematic and theatrical sophistication. Vintage photographs from t

item.title: US and China Discuss International AI Safety Protocols Amid Technology Race
item.description: The Washington Post's AI & Tech Brief highlights ongoing discussions between the U.S. and China regarding international safety standards for artificial intelligence. President Donald Trump’s administration is actively engaging with Chinese officials to create collaborative safety protocols, reflecting concerns over the global implications of AI development. The article examines whether China is closing the gap with U.S. AI capabilities and argues that while leading-edge AI breakthroughs draw attention, long-term impacts may rely more on widespread adoption and safety measures rather than frontier technologies alone. Additionally, the report covers the inaugural Health Council dinner hosted by WP Intelligence, where special guests from the administration discussed integrating AI into the healthcare system. Key points included the importance of system prompts to guide chatbot behavior and public sentiment, with seven in ten Americans opposing the placement of data centers in their commun

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Anthony Perkins in Orson Welles’ The Trial (1962)
item.description: On the set of Orson Welles’ 1962 adaptation of Franz Kafka’s The Trial, Anthony Perkins experienced what he later described as his 'greatest professional pride'. Behind-the-scenes photographs by Nicolas Tikhomiroff reveal moments of laughter and camaraderie between Perkins and Welles despite the film’s dark, surreal tone. Welles directed Perkins to approach the role of Josef K. as a black comedy, blending nervousness and stuttering to convey the character’s confusion within an oppressive, bureaucratic world. Some of Perkins’ lines were even dubbed by Welles, challenging the actor to distinguish them, which he could not. The production primarily took place in the massive, abandoned Gare d'Orsay train station in Paris, giving the film its signature vast and haunting visual style. Although a key scene involving an 'electronic brain' and a scientist was cut from the final film, Perkins’ performance is widely regarded as the stabilizing force that kept the abstract, nightmarish production g

item.title: Megan Williams at the 1975 Mad Hatters Contest in Sydney
item.description: In 1975, Australian actress Megan Williams, then just 18, was photographed preparing for the whimsical 'Mad Hatters Contest' at the Sydney Opera House. The event, likely organized for promotional or charity purposes, embraced the playful, avant-garde spirit of mid-1970s Sydney. Williams wore an elaborate, wide-brimmed hat adorned with large fabric flowers, leaves, and decorative wire topped with pearls or beads, reflecting the iconic 'Mad Hatter' theme. In a nod to Alice in Wonderland, she posed with a teapot and teacup, showcasing the charm and charisma that would make her a recognizable figure in Australian entertainment. Following this early appearance, Williams gained fame through major television roles, notably as Alice in 'The Sullivans' and later as Cassie MacLean in the internationally recognized soap opera 'Return to Eden.' Beyond acting, she contributed musically, providing backing vocals on the 1980s hit 'The Honeymoon is Over' by The Cruel Sea. Williams’ life was tragically

item.title: Spotify Adopts Apple HLS Technology for Enhanced Video Podcast Distribution and Monetization
item.description: Spotify announced on May 14, 2026, that it will integrate Apple’s HLS (HTTP Live Streaming) video technology into its platform. This move builds on Apple’s recent implementation in Apple Podcasts and aims to improve video podcast experiences for both creators and listeners. By adopting HLS, Spotify-hosted shows will be able to distribute and monetize video content directly on Apple Podcasts without requiring creators to make changes to their existing workflows. The technology enables automatic real-time adjustment of video quality based on network conditions, ensuring smooth playback whether users are on Wi-Fi or cellular data, with reduced buffering and quality drops. The rollout is planned for later in 2026. In addition to the HLS integration, Spotify introduced new monetization options for creators, including direct sales and expanded partner integrations. The company also simplified video podcast publishing for creators using third-party hosting providers such as Libsyn, Podigee, A

item.title: Licensed counselor sentenced to 2 years for providing ketamine involved in Matthew Perry's death
item.description: Erik Fleming, a licensed drug addiction counselor, has been sentenced to two years in federal prison for his role in the death of actor Matthew Perry. Perry, best known for his role on the popular television show 'Friends,' died from a ketamine overdose after receiving doses administered by Fleming. During the sentencing hearing in Los Angeles, Fleming expressed deep remorse, stating that his chest and heart hurt every day. The case drew widespread media attention due to Perry's celebrity status and the circumstances surrounding his death. Authorities emphasized that the counselor's actions directly contributed to the fatal overdose, highlighting the legal responsibility of licensed professionals in handling controlled substances. The sentence reflects the court's decision to hold Fleming accountable while also serving as a warning to other healthcare providers about the consequences of improper drug administration. Perry's family and fans have reacted to the sentencing with a mixture

item.title: GOP Lawmakers Worry Trump-Focused Midterm Strategy Could Backfire in Competitive Districts
