This change equates to an extra £8.40 per year, or about 40% more than expected, affecting millions of existing customers who signed contracts based on the lower figure. The move has sparked widespread frustration, with customers feeling misled about future costs. Ofcom, the UK's communications regulator, expressed disappointment and reminded providers of their duty to treat customers fairly, though it stopped short of declaring the action illegal. Consumer advocates like Martin Lewis have criticized the hike as making a mockery of consumer protections. Many affected users are now considering switching pr

item.title: Concerns Raised About Legality and Safety of Proposed Nuclear Regulations
item.description: A coalition of 13 organizations, including Beyond Nuclear and the Nuclear Information & Resource Service, has filed comments to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC), warning that recent nuclear policy proposals under the Trump administration may violate federal law. The so-called 'Rubber-Stamp Rule' seeks to allow reactor designs approved by the Department of Energy (DOE) or Department of Defense (DOD) to bypass NRC safety reviews, a practice previously prohibited by amendments to the Atomic Energy Act in 1974. State attorneys general and advocacy groups have raised concerns that exempting 'pilot reactors' from NRC licensing and environmental review could compromise public safety. Experts, including Paul Gunter of Beyond Nuclear and Tim Judson of NIRS, argue that this approach undermines the NRC’s regulatory authority, favoring production agendas over safety. They emphasize that civilian reactors cannot rely on military reactor safety standards, which often accept higher risk l

item.title: Mets benefit from umpire collision as Tigers’ run is stopped at home
item.description: During a May 12, 2026, game at Citi Field in New York, the New York Mets secured a significant break against the Detroit Tigers that helped them maintain a lead and ultimately win 10-2. Despite a challenging season with a record of 16-25 and a high payroll of $334.8 million, the Mets capitalized on an unusual event. In the fifth inning, Detroit’s Riley Greene singled to right field, allowing teammate Colt Keith to advance to third base. Attempting to score, Keith collided briefly with third-base umpire Rob Drake, who fell to the ground. The momentary delay allowed Mets pitcher Freddy Peralta to throw Keith out at home plate, ending the inning and preventing a potential Tigers rally. Following this incident, the Mets scored three runs in both the sixth and eighth innings while their bullpen shut out Detroit for the remainder of the game. This victory marked a turnaround for the Mets, who have won six of their last ten games after enduring a 12-game losing streak earlier in the season. T

item.title: Tom Crean and the Sled Dog Puppies of the Endurance Expedition
item.description: This historic photograph, captured by Frank Hurley in 1915, shows Irish explorer Tom Crean holding a litter of sled dog puppies during Ernest Shackleton’s Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition (1914–1917). The puppies, born on January 7, 1915, were the offspring of two Canadian sled dogs named Samson and Sally. Crean, serving as a key dog handler on the expedition, was known for his deep care and attachment to the animals, which were vital for hauling sledges across the Antarctic ice. Tragically, after the expedition ship Endurance was crushed by ice and supplies ran low, the crew was forced to make the heartbreaking decision to euthanize the remaining dogs to survive. Crean reportedly performed this task himself to minimize the animals’ suffering. Despite the grim outcome, the image highlights both Crean’s strength and compassion, symbolizing the human-animal bond amid extreme conditions. Today, the photograph remains an emblem of Crean’s character, and a statue in Annascaul, County Ker

item.title: The Journey of Skittles: From British Invention to Global Favorite
item.description: Skittles, often perceived as a quintessentially American candy, actually originated in Britain. Introduced in 1974 and named after a traditional British lawn game, Skittles spent its first five years quietly circulating in the UK before arriving in the United States in 1979. The Wrigley Company acquired production and distribution rights in North America by 1982, launching the candy into mass popularity. Known for its chewy center, colorful candy shell, and fruit flavors, Skittles experienced a notable flavor change in 2013 when green shifted from lime to green apple, sparking widespread online debate. The original lime flavor returned in 2021. Skittles' iconic 'Taste the Rainbow' campaign, started in 1994, thrives on unusual and imaginative advertising that consistently surprises audiences. In 2008, Mars, Inc. acquired Wrigley, further expanding the Skittles line with flavors like Tropical, Sour, and Wild Berry while keeping the classic formula intact. In 2015, the brand created all-w

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Ava Gardner in ‘The Little Hut’ (1957)
item.description: Ava Gardner captivated audiences with her timeless beauty and charm during the filming of the 1957 romantic comedy 'The Little Hut.' In the film, she portrayed Lady Susan Ashlow, an elegant and desirable woman caught in a playful love triangle with her neglectful husband, played by Stewart Granger, and his witty friend, portrayed by David Niven. Set on a deserted tropical island following a shipwreck, the movie features sun-drenched scenes that highlight Gardner's sophisticated style, from flowing gowns to minimalist island attire. While the film is a light-hearted comedy of manners, it showcases Gardner at the peak of her Hollywood allure, combining sultry sophistication with effortless charisma. The article includes a collection of portraits capturing Gardner's magnetic screen presence and iconic glamour during the production of the film, emphasizing her lasting impact as one of Hollywood's most captivating leading ladies.

item.title: Georgia GOP Plans Redistricting That Could Target Black-Majority Districts Before 2028
item.description: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp has announced a special legislative session to redraw congressional maps ahead of the 2028 elections. The move follows a recent Supreme Court ruling in Louisiana, which allows Republican-controlled Southern legislatures to revisit district boundaries. While Kemp has ruled out changes for the upcoming 2026 elections due to logistical and legal concerns, Republicans are planning to reshape districts before 2028. One of the key targets is the southwest Georgia district represented by Democratic U.S. Rep. Sanford Bishop, a rare rural Black-majority seat in the Deep South. The GOP sees an opportunity to strengthen its hold in Georgia's congressional delegation, which currently has a 9-5 Republican advantage. Strategists are also considering metro Atlanta districts, though this carries the risk of overextending and diluting Republican influence. Timing the session is delicate, as the legislature will convene during Atlanta's preparations for the World Cup and inte

item.title: The Story of the 'iPhone Girl' and Her Brief Viral Fame in 2008
item.description: In August 2008, shortly after the launch of the iPhone 3G, a British man named Mark Mitchell received a newly purchased iPhone that unexpectedly contained three photos of a young Chinese factory worker. The images depicted a cheerful young woman in a pink-and-white uniform posing with packaged iPhones, likely taken during a camera test on the Foxconn assembly line in Shenzhen, China. These photos accidentally remained on the device when it was shipped. Mark shared them on the MacRumors forum, and the pictures quickly went viral online, with users dubbing her the 'iPhone Girl.' The story captured international attention, appearing on outlets like NBC News, Wired, Gizmodo, TIME, and Chinese media. While many found the images charming, some expressed concern for her job security. Foxconn confirmed her employment was safe, emphasizing the photos were accidental test shots. Her identity was protected, and she did not seek publicity. The incident highlighted the human side of large-scale man

item.title: Henry Giroux on Resisting the Erosion of Empathy and Democratic Values Under Trump
item.description: In this opinion piece, author Henry A. Giroux argues that the Trump administration has accelerated a dangerous moral decline in American society. He contends that Trumpism thrives in a culture where conscience is deliberately dismantled, compassion is viewed as weakness, and cruelty is normalized as a governing principle. Giroux traces these issues to decades of neoliberal policies that prioritized self-interest, inequality, and the erosion of public goods, creating conditions for authoritarian tendencies to flourish. The article criticizes the weaponization of anti-empathy rhetoric, including statements from figures like Elon Musk, and highlights the inversion of values where exclusion and violence are reframed as virtues. Giroux points to specific examples such as U.S. military actions in Iran, including a reported strike on a school that killed numerous children, and the muted public response focused more on economic impacts than human lives. He condemns the complicity of media inst

item.title: Trump Prepares Contingency Letter for Vice President JD Vance During China Visit
item.description: Senior Director for Counterterrorism Sebastian Gorka revealed that President Donald Trump has prepared a letter for Vice President JD Vance to be stored in the Resolute Desk, in the unlikely event that something happens to Trump during his official visit to China. The disclosure was made during a podcast interview with Miranda Devine, where concerns about possible threats to Trump's safety, including covert methods such as poisoning or illness, were discussed. Gorka emphasized that Trump is considered safe and that global leaders are aware of the consequences of harming him. Although questions about potential covert threats were raised, Gorka only referred vaguely to existing security protocols. The letter is described as a formal instrument of authority intended to communicate the seriousness of international consequences to other nations. This precaution highlights the administration's focus on continuity of leadership and the Vice President's role in such scenarios. While the discus

item.title: Trump Biographer Suggests Marco Rubio Could Be Fired for Outshining the President
item.description: According to Trump biographer Michael Wolff, President Donald Trump may be preparing to fire Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Wolff argues that Rubio's competence and consistent presence in the office make him stand out in contrast to Trump, which Wolff calls a 'fatal flaw' in the Trump administration. Speaking on The Daily Beast's 'Inside Trump's Head,' Wolff explained that in Trump's worldview, any subordinate who appears more capable or diligent than him risks termination. Rubio is highlighted as the only cabinet member who reliably shows up for work and effectively manages his responsibilities, a trait that paradoxically could jeopardize his position. This speculation comes amid reports of other recent departures from Trump's cabinet, including FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, and Attorney General Pam Bondi. Wolff's comments also follow Trump's lukewarm proposal of a Vance-Rubio ticket for the 2028 election, further fueling speculation about in

item.title: Streamer claims self-defense in Nashville shooting following altercation over racial slurs
item.description: A Nashville-based livestreamer known as 'Chud the Builder,' whose real name is Dalton Eatherly, claimed he shot a Black man in self-defense after an altercation involving racial slurs. The incident occurred one day after Eatherly was arrested at a restaurant for allegedly refusing to pay for his meal and violating a no-livestreaming request. Reports indicate that during the arrest, Eatherly used racial slurs, became disruptive, and subsequently faced federal scrutiny, including the seizure of his devices, firearms, and frozen bank accounts. Eatherly defended himself on social media, claiming he was wrongly targeted and that certain actions, such as using racial language among White people, were not illegal. A livestream showed him explaining to police that the altercation escalated after verbal provocations, resulting in him shooting the man in self-defense. He also reportedly grazed himself in the incident. Authorities are investigating the circumstances of the shooting, while Eatherl

item.title: Republicans Respond to Trump's Remarks About Americans' Financial Concerns
item.description: On May 12, 2026, President Donald Trump publicly stated that Americans' financial situations were not a consideration in his approach to ending the conflict with Iran. His comments, made outside the White House and later reinforced during an interview with ABC News, emphasized that preventing Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons was the only factor that mattered to him. The remarks quickly drew criticism from Democrats and were widely reported in the media. In response, several Republican lawmakers attempted to defend the president, though their statements were often hesitant or contradictory. Senator Cynthia Lummis expressed doubt and claimed Trump does care, while Representative Troy Nehls highlighted Trump's general concern for Americans, particularly regarding fuel prices and affordability. Similarly, Representatives James Comer and Rich McCormick insisted Trump focuses on economic issues like jobs and the cost of living, despite acknowledging the president's exact words. Senators R

item.title: Elvis Presley’s 1966 Dodge D200 Crew Cab and His Circle G Ranch Life
item.description: In 1967, Elvis Presley purchased the Circle G Ranch in Horn Lake, Mississippi, as a personal retreat and later used it as a honeymoon cottage after marrying Priscilla. To manage the ranch and care for his 25 horses, Elvis acquired practical vehicles, including a 1966 Dodge D200 Sweptline with a rare four-door crew cab configuration. At the time, such trucks were uncommon for personal use and mainly used by construction or utility crews. Elvis not only bought one truck but a fleet of Dodge and Chevrolet pickups for himself, his close friends known as the 'Memphis Mafia,' and ranch staff. Many of these trucks featured the Circle G logo, marking them as part of the ranch operations. During this period, Elvis frequently traded his flashy Cadillacs for these working trucks and was often seen around Horn Lake driving the D200 in cowboy attire, hauling horse trailers, and moving supplies. Friends recalled this era as a particularly happy time, as the ranch offered him normalcy away from Holly

item.title: Jen Psaki Highlights Past Comments by FBI Director Kash Patel on Alcohol Use
item.description: MS NOW host Jen Psaki drew attention to FBI Director Kash Patel's previous public statements about his drinking habits, highlighting a series of clips where Patel openly discussed alcohol consumption. The clips show Patel joking about being labeled an alcoholic if he ever faced a Senate confirmation hearing, including moments where he drank beer during interviews and public appearances. This comes amid recent questions about Patel's alcohol use following reports of alleged misconduct and viral footage of him drinking in celebratory settings with the U.S. Men's Hockey team after their Olympic gold medal win in Milan, Italy. Psaki pointed out that while Patel has denied specific stories about heavy drinking since taking office, his prior comments and public behavior provide insight into his past drinking habits. The coverage has sparked discussion about transparency and accountability for public officials, especially those in high-profile government positions.

item.title: Jessica Lange: Early Portraits by Giancarlo Botti in Paris, 1976
item.description: In December 1976, the Italian photographer Giancarlo Botti captured a striking series of portraits of American actress Jessica Lange in Paris. At the age of 27, Lange was on the verge of international fame, visiting France to promote her film debut in the 1976 remake of King Kong, a role that earned her a Golden Globe for New Star of the Year. The 1970s marked a transformative period in Lange’s life, as she evolved from a bohemian art student and model into a globally recognized, though initially controversial, actress. After leaving the University of Minnesota, Lange moved to Paris in 1971 to study mime theater under Étienne Decroux and joined the Opéra-Comique as a dancer. Her discovery by fashion illustrator Antonio Lopez led to a modeling career with Wilhelmina agency. Returning to New York in 1973, she balanced modeling with work as a waitress. Lange’s film debut as Dwan in King Kong initially received harsh critical reviews despite the film’s box-office success. Nevertheless, she

item.title: Tarot Readers Turn to AI for Card Interpretations and What It Reveals About Seeking Emotional Support from Chatbots
item.description: A recent study highlights the growing intersection between artificial intelligence and traditional occult practices, specifically how tarot readers are increasingly using AI tools to interpret card readings. Tarot, which originated as a Renaissance card game before evolving into a tool for self-reflection and guidance, involves drawing cards and interpreting their symbolic meanings based on personal context and life circumstances. Researchers interviewed 12 tarot practitioners who use AI in two primary ways: to obtain quick interpretations that reduce the cognitive effort of analyzing ambiguous card combinations, and to generate alternative perspectives that challenge their own biases and assumptions. While AI provides confident, sycophantic-style responses that can boost users' certainty, it often lacks deep personal context. Some readers appreciate AI's apparent objectivity and use it to avoid burdening friends, creating a form of parasocial interaction. The study notes broader socie

item.title: Judy Garland and Young Liza Minnelli: A Glimpse into Their Early Bond
item.description: The relationship between Judy Garland and her daughter Liza Minnelli is one of the most well-documented and emotionally resonant in Hollywood history. Born in 1946 to Judy and director Vincente Minnelli, Liza entered a world of cinema during a peak period of her mother's career at MGM. By the age of three, Liza made her first appearance on screen, walking alongside her mother in the 1949 film 'In the Good Old Summertime.' During the late 1940s, Judy often brought Liza to the studio, where archival photos capture moments of Liza sitting on Judy’s lap in the makeup chair or playing with costumes on set, notably during the production of 'The Pirate.' Judy frequently treated Liza more as a companion than a traditional child, even dressing her in miniature versions of her own stage outfits. Despite the pressures of Hollywood, health challenges, and Liza’s own emerging career, the mother-daughter bond remained close, with Liza often describing her mother as her best friend. Their connection

item.title: Trump Visits China Amid Stalled Iran Ceasefire and Rising Middle East Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has arrived in China as negotiations for a ceasefire with Iran remain stalled, amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. Trump is expected to urge Chinese President Xi Jinping to pressure Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route, and to help broker a peace deal. Meanwhile, military operations continue in the region, including Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon that killed at least 12 people, and Hezbollah drone attacks targeting UN positions. The conflict has significantly impacted the global economy, with US military actions in Iran costing an estimated $29 billion, while diesel prices in the Midwest have reached record highs, affecting transport and food costs. Oil reserves worldwide are rapidly shrinking, with the International Energy Agency warning of potential future price spikes. Human rights concerns have also surfaced, with Iranian activists Narges Mohammadi and Nasrin Sotoudeh temporarily released from custody due to heal

item.title: Historical Inequities Drive Detroit’s Water Affordability Challenges
item.description: Starting in July 2026, Detroit residents will face higher water and sewer rates, with increases of 5.8% and 4.26%, respectively, following similar hikes in 2025. These rises exacerbate an already severe water affordability crisis, particularly for low-income households. DWSD’s average water bill, $87.54 per month, can consume up to 25% of income for families living below the poverty line, far exceeding the EPA’s recommended 4.5% affordability threshold. A major driver of the crisis is the historical regionalization of Detroit’s wastewater system. Originally designed to serve the city alone, the system was expanded in the mid-20th century to accommodate suburban growth, white flight, and stricter state wastewater regulations. Cost-sharing arrangements, including the 1999 '83/17 split,' disproportionately allocate stormwater management costs to Detroit residents, despite them representing only 23% of the broader GLWA customer base. These financial pressures have led to widespread water s

item.title: Tata Motors' Domestic Vehicle Growth May Help Offset Jaguar Land Rover Margin Challenges in Q4
item.description: Tata Motors is set to release its March quarter results, with analysts forecasting strong performance from its domestic passenger vehicle (PV) segment. The growth is expected to be driven by a 37% increase in sales volumes and a 7-8% rise in average selling prices due to an improved product mix. Brokerages, including Kotak Equities and Motilal Oswal Financial, anticipate revenue growth of approximately 47% year-on-year for Tata Motors' India PV business, alongside modest sequential margin improvements aided by operating leverage. Increased demand for SUVs and electric vehicles, along with premiumization trends in the domestic market, are contributing to better realizations. Meanwhile, the company’s luxury arm, Jaguar Land Rover (JLR), continues to face margin pressure despite a sequential recovery in volumes after a previous cyber incident. JLR volumes were still down 14.5% year-on-year, and although EBITDA margins are projected to improve to 7.7% from 0.7%, they remain well below last

item.title: Everyday Life in 1960s Mallorca Through 30 Vintage Photographs
item.description: In the 1960s, Mallorca underwent a significant transformation from a quiet Mediterranean island to a popular European holiday destination. With its stunning beaches, rugged mountains, and sunny climate, the island began attracting growing numbers of tourists, particularly from Britain and Germany, as package holidays became more affordable. Traditional fishing villages such as Magaluf, Palma Nova, and Cala d’Or evolved into bustling resorts, while life in the inland villages maintained its local charm. The decade was marked by leisurely beach days, simple chiringuitos serving sangria, and lively night entertainment in newly emerging beach clubs. Celebrities, artists, and writers were drawn to picturesque towns like Deià and Valldemossa, seeking both inspiration and relaxation. The era saw the rise of bikinis, rapid infrastructure expansion, and a mix of old Mallorcan traditions with modern cosmopolitan influences. Photographer Kai Heinrich captured these moments in 1968, showcasing a s

item.title: Beijing Residents Express Opposition to Trump Seeking Chinese Help on Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump arrived in China on May 13, 2026, ahead of a highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Reports indicate that Trump intends to seek Beijing's assistance in pressuring Iran regarding the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route. CNN reporters on the ground in Beijing observed strong opposition from local residents toward U.S. involvement in the Iran conflict. Residents such as Mr. Liu criticized Trump for previous conflicts, including the trade war and the U.S.-Iran tensions. Others, like Mr. Li and Ms. Yuan, emphasized that China should not intervene in foreign wars, citing the nation's diplomatic principles. Trump's visit reportedly began awkwardly, as he was greeted by lower-ranking officials instead of Xi, an optics issue described as 'humiliating' by Mary L. Trump. Observers note that the summit will focus heavily on securing deals and garnering support for U.S. policy in Iran, yet local sentiment in Beijing appears resist

item.title: A Look Back at Women’s Fashion in the 1970s
item.description: The 1970s were a transformative era in women’s fashion, blending self-expression, cultural shifts, and bold experimentation. Early in the decade, the influence of the late 1960s was still evident, with flowing maxi dresses, tie-dye shirts, and bell-bottom jeans dominating casual wear. As the decade progressed, the rise of disco culture introduced glamorous sequined outfits, satin wrap dresses, and platform shoes that made a statement both on the dance floor and in popular culture. Beyond leisurewear, the 1970s also marked a shift in workplace attire for women. Tailored pantsuits, blazers, and structured outfits became increasingly common, signaling the early stages of what would later be known as “power dressing.” The decade’s fashion was defined by a fearless approach to patterns, textures, and silhouettes, allowing women to explore individuality through clothing. These 35 vintage snapshots serve as a nostalgic window into the decade, capturing both everyday style and iconic looks, an

item.title: University of Nebraska Develops Early Detection Test for Andes Hantavirus
item.description: As a rare Andes hantavirus outbreak occurred on a cruise ship, the United States faced a shortage of early diagnostic tools for the virus. The University of Nebraska Medical Center rapidly developed a PCR test capable of detecting the virus before severe symptoms appear. This test is crucial for early patient care and preventing further transmission. Unlike the CDC's antibody tests, which only detect infection once symptoms are advanced, this new PCR test can identify low viral concentrations in blood, giving doctors the ability to begin supportive treatment sooner. The Nebraska lab collaborated with a research team in New Mexico to obtain non-infectious genetic material of the Andes virus and necessary reagents, allowing them to validate the test over a weekend. While the Andes virus has a fatality rate of roughly 35%, early supportive care such as fluid and breathing management can significantly improve survival. Although there are no immediate antiviral drugs, the test gives health

item.title: US House Committee Seeks Instructure Testimony Following Multiple Canvas Data Breaches
item.description: U.S. House lawmakers are pressing Instructure, the company behind the Canvas educational platform, to provide detailed answers regarding recent cyberattacks that compromised the personal data of millions of students worldwide. The House Homeland Security Committee, led by Representative Andrew Garbarino, is investigating the breaches due to the company’s repeated security failures. Lawmakers are requesting testimony from Instructure CEO Steve Daly to clarify how hackers were able to exploit the same vulnerability twice, the types of data stolen, and the company’s approach to notifying affected schools. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has been involved in managing the response. Instructure recently disclosed that it had reached an agreement with the ShinyHunters hacking group, which claimed to have deleted the stolen data. Security experts caution that paying hackers may encourage further attacks, as stolen data can be retained for future extortion. The

item.title: The Beach Boys in the 1960s: Their Rise, Hits, and Musical Evolution
item.description: The Beach Boys, formed in 1961 in Hawthorne, California, became one of the most influential American bands of the 1960s. The core members included brothers Brian, Carl, and Dennis Wilson, their cousin Mike Love, and friend Al Jardine. They started with surf-rock hits celebrating California beaches, cars, and romance, with early successes like 'Surfin'' (1961), 'Surfin' U.S.A.' (1963), 'Surfer Girl,' and 'Little Deuce Coupe.' The band released multiple albums per year and maintained a strong touring presence, becoming America's answer to the British Invasion with numerous Top 40 hits. In late 1964, Brian Wilson stopped touring to focus on songwriting and production, leading to their artistic peak in 1966 with the groundbreaking album Pet Sounds. This record featured sophisticated vocal harmonies, orchestral arrangements, and innovative instrumentation, influencing The Beatles' Sgt. Pepper's. The single 'Good Vibrations' further showcased their experimental 'pocket symphony' style. Later

item.title: DHS Launches Audit of Warehouse-Based Immigration Detention Program Under Kristi Noem
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security's inspector general has initiated an audit into a controversial $38 billion program that converted commercial warehouses into immigration detention centers during Kristi Noem's tenure as DHS Secretary, with advisement from Corey Lewandowski. The audit aims to review all warehouse acquisitions made by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), building on ongoing investigations regarding DHS contract management and the role of political appointees. Last winter, ICE acquired 11 warehouses in just a few months, funded through the One Big Beautiful Bill, reportedly paying 11-13% above market value for facilities designed to house up to 8,000 detainees. Critics have argued that repurposing existing jail facilities could have been a faster and more cost-effective alternative. Approximately 50 contractors received $1.7 billion since January 2025, including several firms with no prior federal contracts or experience in immigration detention. After being

item.title: NC Congressman Under Ethics Investigation Allegedly Made Female Staffer Uncomfortable
item.description: Rep. Chuck Edwards, a Republican congressman from North Carolina, is under investigation by the House Ethics Committee for alleged inappropriate behavior toward a female staffer. According to reporting from Axios, Edwards sent the young staffer a guilt-inducing text after she declined his dinner invitation, expressing disappointment that the situation had become 'complicated.' The staffer reportedly experienced repeated unwanted compliments, late-night outings, casino trips, and a Las Vegas vacation organized by Edwards months after she left his office. During her going-away party, Edwards allegedly read a self-written poem while a slideshow of their interactions played, which made the staffer and other colleagues uncomfortable. She reportedly feared career retaliation if she complained. Edwards also visited the staffer in the hospital during her appendicitis treatment and sent flowers to her hotel room during a later trip, which the staffer found uncomfortable. Edwards has denied any

item.title: Jason Collins, trailblazing NBA player, passes away at 47
item.description: Jason Collins, the first NBA player to publicly come out as gay, has passed away at the age of 47. Known for his trailblazing role in professional sports, Collins made history in 2013 when he became the first openly gay athlete active in one of the major American professional sports leagues. His announcement was widely regarded as a pivotal moment for LGBTQ+ visibility and acceptance in sports. Throughout his career, Collins played for several NBA teams, including the New Jersey Nets, Memphis Grizzlies, and Boston Celtics, and was respected both for his athletic ability and his integrity. Beyond the court, Collins became an advocate for LGBTQ+ rights, speaking openly about the challenges faced by gay athletes and promoting inclusion. His death has been met with tributes from fellow athletes, fans, and public figures who recognize his courage and the lasting impact he had on sports culture. Collins' legacy is remembered not only for his accomplishments in basketball but also for the doo

item.title: How Understanding Your Own Knowledge Gaps Can Improve Learning and Teaching
item.description: Many people overestimate how much they actually know about everyday objects and processes. Studies show that individuals often cannot accurately recall familiar logos, coins, or the mechanics of bicycles, despite believing they understand them well. This overconfidence stems from confusing visible information with internal knowledge, misjudging the level of detail they truly understand, and being unaware of how their own knowledge shapes perception. For instance, when asked to tap out a song for others to identify, participants vastly overestimated how recognizable it would be. This phenomenon, known as metaknowledge—the awareness of one's own understanding—affects teaching as well. Experts may struggle to convey knowledge effectively because they internalize information so deeply that they overlook beginners' challenges. To correct these knowledge gaps, individuals should actively use and explain what they know, which reveals limits in understanding and fosters intellectual humility.

item.title: The Life and Designs of Frederick Starke in Post-War British Fashion
item.description: Frederick Starke (1904–1988) was a highly influential British wholesale couturier whose work shaped post-war fashion in the United Kingdom. Born into a Jewish family with a strong tradition in the clothing trade, he founded Frederick Starke Ltd. in 1933 and rose to prominence after World War II. Based on Bruton Street in London's prestigious Mayfair district, Starke was renowned for his elegant, feminine, and impeccably tailored ready-to-wear garments. His creations bridged the gap between haute couture and high-end ready-to-wear, making sophisticated fashion accessible to society women and celebrities alike. Starke’s designs embodied the glamour of the 1950s and 1960s, featuring luxurious fabrics, flattering silhouettes, full skirts, and refined cocktail dresses. He was a founding member of the Model House Group and later the Fashion House Group, helping to elevate the standards and visibility of British wholesale couture. The extensive photography of his work, captured by prominent p

item.title: Daily Pill May Help Maintain Weight Loss After Injectable Treatments
item.description: Recent studies suggest that transitioning from weight-loss injections to a daily oral medication may help individuals sustain weight loss over the long term. Weight-loss injections, such as those containing GLP-1 receptor agonists, have been effective for reducing weight in many patients. However, maintaining results after stopping injections can be challenging. Researchers found that patients who switched to a daily pill with similar effects experienced less weight regain compared to those who stopped medication entirely. The approach may offer a more convenient, sustainable, and less invasive alternative to injections, potentially improving adherence and overall success rates. Experts emphasize the importance of combining medication with lifestyle interventions, including a balanced diet and regular exercise, to maximize benefits. While the findings are promising, further clinical trials are needed to confirm long-term outcomes and safety. This development could mark a shift in obesi

item.title: WhatsApp Launches Incognito Chat Feature for Private Conversations with Meta AI
item.description: WhatsApp has introduced a new feature called Incognito Chat, designed to let users interact with Meta AI while maintaining strong privacy protections. The chats leverage WhatsApp's Private Processing system, which ensures that Meta cannot access the content of user conversations with the AI. This addresses concerns about balancing advanced AI capabilities with the platform's longstanding commitment to end-to-end encryption. Unlike typical incognito modes on other AI platforms that primarily separate user data from training models, WhatsApp's approach aims to prevent even Meta from viewing the exchanges. Conversations are ephemeral by default, disappearing after the session ends, and processing occurs within Trusted Execution Environments in Meta's secure cloud infrastructure. Meta is inviting third-party audits to verify the system's security. The rollout also includes a Side Chat feature, allowing users to consult Meta AI about ongoing group or individual chats without sharing sensiti

item.title: Trump's $10 Billion IRS Lawsuit May Be Settled by DOJ
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is reportedly discussing the possibility of settling President Donald Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service. The suit, filed in January 2026, alleges that the IRS failed to prevent the public disclosure of Trump's 2019 tax returns. The case raises potential conflicts of interest because the IRS is part of the executive branch, which Trump leads. Political commentators have criticized the potential settlement as an attempt to use government authority to benefit Trump personally. Former White House economic official Bharat Ramamurti called it a 'massive scam,' while Jonathan Cohn referred to it as 'record-breaking corruption.' Activists and political strategists, including Hemant Mehta and Donna Brazile, expressed concern that the settlement could reward Trump unfairly and undermine accountability for government leaks. The discussions come amid broader scrutiny of the IRS, which has been facing layoffs and restructuring that could

item.title: JWST Produces Most Detailed Map of the Cosmic Web Tracing Back to When the Universe Was 1 Billion Years Old
item.description: Astronomers using NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) have produced the highest-resolution map yet of the universe's cosmic web, the vast network of dark matter and gas filaments that connects galaxies across immense distances. Led by researchers at the University of California, Riverside, the team utilized data from the COSMOS-Web survey, which analyzed over 164,000 galaxies. This allowed them to trace the large-scale structure back to when the universe was approximately one billion years old. The cosmic web consists of filaments, sheets, and enormous voids, forming the underlying skeleton of the cosmos. JWST's superior infrared sensitivity and resolution enabled the detection of many more faint galaxies and more precise distance measurements compared to previous Hubble observations, revealing finer details that were previously blurred. The survey covers an area roughly the size of three full Moons and provides insights into galaxy evolution within cluster and filamentary environ

item.title: Authorities Charge Ship Operator and Worker After Baltimore Bridge Collapse Kills Multiple People
item.description: The Baltimore Bridge collapse has led to criminal charges against a ship operator and one of their employees. The incident resulted in several fatalities and caused significant disruption to local traffic and infrastructure. Authorities conducted an investigation into the circumstances surrounding the collapse, determining that human error and negligence may have contributed to the tragedy. Legal proceedings are underway, and the defendants face potential prison sentences and fines if found guilty. The collapse has raised questions about safety protocols, bridge maintenance, and monitoring procedures for large vessels operating near critical infrastructure. Government officials and safety regulators are reviewing policies to prevent similar accidents in the future. Local communities affected by the collapse are demanding accountability and improved safety measures to ensure that such a disaster does not happen again. The case has attracted national attention, highlighting the risks ass

item.title: Pope Appoints Immigration-Critical Pastor to Lead Naples Diocese in Florida
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has appointed Father Emilio Biosca Agüero, known for his criticism of the Trump administration's deportation policies, as the new Bishop of Naples, Florida. Biosca Agüero has led the Shrine of the Sacred Heart in Washington, D.C., since 2019, serving a predominantly Salvadoran congregation of approximately 5,600 members. The pastor has been outspoken about the impact of intensified immigration enforcement, which has resulted in over 40 parishioners being detained or deported since August, including an usher, a man preparing for marriage, and a confirmation student. Service attendance at his parish dropped 20 percent due to fear of public visibility. In interviews, Biosca Agüero criticized Immigration and Customs Enforcement for targeting Hispanic individuals rather than focusing on violent criminals. The Naples Diocese covers a conservative region of southwest Florida that President Trump won decisively in 2024, and it has a rapidly growing Hispanic Catholic population. Th

item.title: Joyce DeWitt’s Role as Janet Wood in “Three’s Company” Highlighted Through Vintage Portraits
item.description: Joyce DeWitt played the role of Janet Wood on ABC’s popular sitcom “Three’s Company” from 1977 to 1984. Her character, known for her iconic brunette bob, was the pragmatic and reliable roommate who balanced the antics of her more eccentric roommates, Chrissy Snow and Jack Tripper. Janet’s grounded and sensible nature made her the show’s ‘straight woman,’ providing essential contrast to the comedic chaos around her. Working at the Arcade Flower Shop, she was portrayed as career-oriented and responsible, often standing up to the landlords or guiding the more naive roommates. Over the years, her style evolved from a short pixie cut and casual wardrobe to a chic shag haircut and polished professional outfits, reflecting the trends of the late 1970s and early 1980s. The chemistry between DeWitt and John Ritter was based on a deep, platonic friendship, with Janet often acting as a confidante and protector for Jack and the other roommates. Originally cast in the second pilot, DeWitt replaced

item.title: Northwest Redistricting Challenges and Potential Maps Ahead of 2026 Elections
item.description: While much of the national attention on gerrymandering has focused on battleground and heavily redistricted states, the Northwest faces unique constraints. Smaller states like Alaska and Wyoming have only one congressional seat each, making redistricting irrelevant. Montana regained a second district this decade, but the split largely follows historical east-west lines, offering minimal partisan advantage. Idaho, with two firmly Republican districts, presents little opportunity for Democrats, though a potential third seat after the next census could change dynamics. Oregon’s current map slightly favors Democrats with five out of six districts, yet the 5th district remains competitive. Republican control could theoretically balance Oregon’s map to three Democratic and three Republican seats, but such control is unlikely. Washington State presents the most theoretical potential for partisan map changes, with proposals circulating for a 9-Democrat, 1-Republican scenario heavily centered o

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Wardrobe Polaroids of Catherine O’Hara on SCTV in 1983
item.description: In 1983, Catherine O’Hara, the celebrated Canadian actress, was a key performer on the iconic sketch comedy show Second City Television (SCTV). A newly surfaced collection of wardrobe Polaroids provides a rare behind-the-scenes look at her work during that period. These photographs, taken primarily for continuity and makeup testing, document the variety of eccentric characters she portrayed, including the fan-favorite Lola Heatherton. The collection also includes images with her frequent collaborator John Candy, capturing their dynamic performances and the collaborative spirit of SCTV at its peak. The Polaroids highlight the meticulous work of the wardrobe and makeup departments, led by designers Linda Matheson and Juul Haalmeyer, who ensured that characters maintained consistent appearances across filming sessions. Many images feature alternate looks and makeup tests, some of which have circulated through archives, offering fans and researchers a glimpse into the creative process behi

item.title: Republicans Struggle to Address Affordability Amid Internal Controversies
item.description: As the 2026 midterm elections approach, Republicans face growing criticism for failing to focus on affordability, the top concern for American voters. According to Politico, the party is being consumed by self-inflicted controversies that undermine its ability to present concrete solutions. A $1 billion funding request for the Secret Service, including $220 million potentially linked to a Trump White House ballroom renovation, has sparked alarm even among senior GOP leaders. Centrist Republicans, such as Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, have publicly opposed the request, while Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer has criticized the party for prioritizing a presidential project over relief for everyday costs. Efforts like suspending the federal gas tax, previously ridiculed by GOP lawmakers, now face political challenges if they attempt to reverse past stances. House and Senate disputes over a housing bill further delay potential wins for the party, frustrating members like Sen. John Kennedy. Fo

item.title: Democrats Question FBI Director Kash Patel Over Conduct and Allegations During Capitol Hearing
item.description: During a recent Capitol Hill hearing, FBI Director Kash Patel faced rigorous questioning from Democratic lawmakers regarding his personal conduct and professional decisions. The hearing, which focused on the Justice Department budget, included inquiries into Patel's alleged drinking habits and his involvement in investigations of President Donald Trump's political opponents. According to MS NOW reporter Ken Dilanian, Patel has limited support among rank-and-file FBI agents, with many expressing frustration over his approach to congressional hearings. Democrats strategically questioned Patel under oath about allegations reported by The Atlantic, including claims that Patel had been too intoxicated for his security detail to rouse him. While Patel attempted to deflect some questions and accuse certain lawmakers of misconduct, these efforts were largely seen as evasive. The hearing is part of a broader effort by Democrats to hold senior officials accountable and gather testimonies that co

item.title: Senate Republicans question $1 billion funding request for White House security upgrades
item.description: Senate Republicans are expressing concern over a $1 billion funding request included in a broader immigration enforcement package that would, in part, fund security upgrades for President Donald Trump's planned White House ballroom. The proposal, presented by Secret Service Director Sean Curran, allocates $220 million for hardening the White House complex with measures such as bulletproof glass, drone detection technology, chemical filtration systems, and other security enhancements. Another $180 million would go toward a White House visitor screening center, with the remaining $600 million intended for Secret Service training and broader protective measures for the president and other officials. Several Republican senators, including John Curtis and Todd Young, questioned how the $1 billion figure was determined and requested a more detailed breakdown. While lawmakers generally support enhanced security, they are cautious about the total cost, especially amid economic concerns. Democr

item.title: Tallulah Bankhead: Iconic Broadway Actress and Unforgettable Personality
item.description: Tallulah Bankhead (1902–1968) was a remarkable American actress celebrated for her commanding stage presence, gravelly voice, and sharp wit. Born into a prominent political family in Alabama, she broke social norms by pursuing acting, eventually achieving fame on Broadway and London’s West End. Bankhead is most remembered for her standout film role in Alfred Hitchcock’s 'Lifeboat' (1944) and her acclaimed stage performance in 'The Little Foxes.' Beyond her acting prowess, she was known for her free-spirited, flamboyant personality, often greeting people with her signature term “dahling” due to her difficulty remembering names. Her life and career challenged traditional expectations of women in her era, making her not just a celebrated performer but a cultural icon. Vintage portraits of Bankhead capture the magnetic charm, confidence, and daring persona that made her one of the most unforgettable figures in 20th-century theater and cinema.

item.title: History and Charm of Crocodile Rock in Millport, Scotland
item.description: Crocodile Rock, located on Millport Beach in the Isle of Great Cumbrae, Scotland, is a unique natural rock formation that has been painted to resemble a crocodile for over a century. Formed millions of years ago from local geological processes, the rock gained its playful crocodile appearance around 1913–1914 thanks to Robert Brown, a local man credited with painting the rock after noticing its reptile-like shape. The Millport Town Council recognized Brown's work shortly after, cementing its place as a community landmark. Over the years, Crocodile Rock has been maintained and repainted by locals and volunteers, ensuring it remains vibrant and visually striking. It has become a popular spot for photography, especially among families and children who enjoy climbing on the formation, despite its occasional slipperiness. The rock is part of a series of painted rocks on the island, including Lion Rock and Indian Rock, but it remains the most famous. In 2013, the town celebrated the centenar

item.title: Authorities Issue Millions of Warnings to Users Searching for Child Sexual Abuse Material
item.description: Authorities have sent over 70 million warnings to internet users who attempted to access content related to child sexual abuse. These warnings are part of ongoing efforts to prevent the distribution of illegal material and to educate individuals about the serious legal consequences associated with such actions. The initiative reflects a global commitment to protecting children online by combining law enforcement measures, monitoring tools, and public awareness campaigns. While exact details about how the warnings were distributed or the identities of the users remain confidential, the scale of the operation highlights the magnitude of attempts to access illegal content online. Experts emphasize that these warnings are not only a deterrent but also a critical component in reducing the circulation of abusive material. This approach demonstrates the importance of proactive strategies to safeguard vulnerable populations and reinforces the responsibility of internet users to act within the

item.title: Bijou Barrington: Celebrated 1940s American Fashion Icon
item.description: Bijou Barrington was a prominent American fashion model in the 1940s, renowned for her sophisticated and graceful presence. She frequently appeared in major fashion magazines like Harper’s Bazaar and Vogue, where she showcased the works of influential designers such as Claire McCardell, Jay Thorpe, and Nettie Rosenstein. Barrington became a favorite subject for legendary photographers including Louise Dahl-Wolfe, John Rawlings, Horst P. Horst, George Hoyningen-Huene, Toni Frissell, Nina Leen, and Serge Balkin. Her career featured memorable moments, such as a 1942 Arizona photo shoot where editor Diana Vreeland stepped in to model after Barrington suffered heatstroke. Throughout her career, Barrington embodied the refined elegance of mid-century American fashion, appearing in a variety of stylish outfits ranging from sophisticated suits and evening dresses to innovative swimwear and casual ensembles. Her timeless style continues to serve as an inspiration for fashion historians and enth

item.title: DOJ Faces Ethics Inquiry Over Withholding Murder Warrant From Federal Judge
item.description: The Trump administration is under scrutiny for allegedly concealing a murder warrant from a federal judge in Rhode Island, Melissa DuBose, in order to secure the release of an immigrant in detention and then criticize the judge's record. The Department of Homeland Security publicly defended its actions, suggesting that the case represents typical judicial misconduct due to allegedly biased judges appointed during the Biden administration. Critics, including Aaron Reichlin-Melnick from the American Immigration Council, condemned the ongoing attacks on Judge DuBose, emphasizing that ICE instructed DOJ attorneys not to disclose the murder warrant and that the DOJ attorneys complied with this directive. Judge DuBose has since ordered an ethics investigation into the DOJ attorney who failed to provide this critical information. The situation reflects growing tensions over immigration detention policies and transparency in the Justice Department, with courts increasingly challenging the admi

item.title: Reversing Lysosomal Dysfunction Restores Youthful Function in Aged Blood Stem Cells
item.description: Researchers at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai have discovered a method to restore youthful function to aged blood-forming stem cells in mice by repairing lysosomal defects. Lysosomes, the cell's recycling centers, become overactive and damaged as stem cells age, disrupting metabolism and contributing to inflammation and reduced regenerative capacity. The study focused on hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs), which produce all blood and immune cells. Using a vacuolar ATPase inhibitor to slow lysosomal overactivity, scientists successfully rejuvenated old HSCs, improving their ability to generate balanced blood and immune cells, enhance metabolism, and reduce harmful inflammatory signals. Ex vivo treatment of aged stem cells increased their blood-forming potential over eightfold, demonstrating strong regenerative effects. These findings suggest that targeting lysosomal dysfunction could help prevent or treat age-related blood disorders, improve stem cell transplantation in older

item.title: A Visual Journey Through Early 1900s Saturday Evening Post Covers
item.description: In the early 20th century, The Saturday Evening Post became one of America's most beloved magazines, recognized for its striking illustrated covers. From 1900 to the 1920s, these covers offered vivid snapshots of American life, reflecting the optimism, humor, and social changes of the Edwardian and Progressive eras. Renowned illustrators such as J.C. Leyendecker and Norman Rockwell, who began contributing in 1916, brought stories to life with masterful artistry, combining dynamic compositions, bold colors, and relatable characters. The magazine covers depicted a wide range of scenes, from bustling city streets and serene small-town life to romantic and humorous everyday moments. Each illustration captured the spirit of the time, offering readers a weekly window into the joys, aspirations, and transformations of early 20th-century America. Today, these covers are more than art—they are cultural artifacts that preserve the hopes, dreams, and experiences of a rapidly changing nation, show

item.title: PCOS Renamed to PMOS to Reflect Multisystem Health Impacts
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), affecting over 170 million people worldwide, has been officially renamed polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS) following a global consensus study published in The Lancet. The change emphasizes that the condition is not solely gynecological, but a complex multisystem disorder involving endocrine, metabolic, reproductive, dermatological, and psychological health. The new classification aims to improve diagnosis, patient care, and research worldwide by reflecting the hormonal and metabolic complexities previously overlooked. Dr. Melanie Cree, a pediatric endocrinologist at the University of Colorado Anschutz, emphasized that the previous terminology led to stigma and misconceptions, particularly regarding fertility. By adopting PMOS, the medical community hopes to promote earlier diagnosis, holistic care, and reduce fertility-focused stigma. The name change will also update clinical guidelines, medical education, and international disease classi

item.title: Pete Buttigieg Endorses Josh Turek in Iowa Senate Democratic Primary
item.description: Pete Buttigieg, former U.S. Transportation Secretary and winner of the 2020 Iowa Democratic caucuses, has officially endorsed state Rep. Josh Turek in the Iowa Senate Democratic primary, opposing state Sen. Zach Wahls. This endorsement marks a notable shift for Buttigieg, who previously stated he had no plans to intervene in contested primaries. Buttigieg praised Turek for following a campaign strategy similar to his own 2020 Iowa approach, emphasizing outreach to both urban and rural voters and building broad coalitions across party lines. The endorsement could strengthen Turek’s campaign and possibly position Buttigieg to gain a political ally if he pursues another presidential run in 2028. However, it also carries the risk of alienating supporters of Wahls in what is expected to be a competitive race. Buttigieg’s backing aligns him with other prominent Democrats, including Senators Tammy Duckworth, Catherine Cortez Masto, Maggie Hassan, and former Senator Tom Harkin, all of whom are

item.title: Democratic Rep. Huffman Questions Interior Secretary Burgum on Rising Gas Prices During Budget Hearing
item.description: During a House Natural Resources Committee hearing on President Trump's 2027 budget request, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum faced pointed questions from Rep. Jared Huffman (D-CA) regarding rising gas prices. Huffman highlighted that gas prices have increased by an average of $1.40 per gallon from the previous year and referenced a social media post by President Trump about the U.S. benefiting from higher oil prices as the world's top producer. Burgum explained that higher prices could generate more revenue through royalties for states, the federal government, and the American people from production on federal lands. When pressed on whether this is good for American families, Burgum noted he was thrilled that prices were about a dollar lower than during the Biden administration, calling it 'exciting,' and predicted prices would drop soon due to increased supply. Huffman expressed frustration over what he saw as a disconnect, arguing that Americans are not celebrating the spike in prices

item.title: Jack Nicholson's Early Years as an Aspiring Actor in the 1950s
item.description: Before becoming an iconic figure of the 1970s, Jack Nicholson's early years in Los Angeles were marked by persistence and learning. Arriving in 1954 at just 17 to visit his sister, Nicholson decided to stay and pursue acting. He began his career behind the scenes as an office boy and mail carrier at MGM, where he worked alongside animation legends William Hanna and Joseph Barbera. Though offered a screen test early on, Nicholson declined, preferring to hone his craft first. He joined the theater group The Players Ring, studying acting while meeting future collaborators like Robert Towne. By the mid-1950s, he was experimenting with acting techniques, moving away from the prevalent 'method' style toward a more personal approach. His first on-screen appearance came in 1956 on Matinee Theatre, and he made his feature film debut in 1958 with The Cry Baby Killer. Towards the end of the decade, he began collaborating with B-movie producer Roger Corman, appearing in films including The Little

item.title: High Salt Content Found in Popular Sandwiches, Some Equivalent to Multiple Fast-Food Burgers
item.description: A recent analysis has revealed that several widely sold sandwiches contain dangerously high levels of salt, with one sandwich approaching the salt content of nearly five McDonald's burgers. Health experts warn that excessive salt intake is linked to increased risks of high blood pressure, heart disease, and other cardiovascular issues. Consumers are being urged to check nutritional labels carefully and consider healthier alternatives when making food choices. The findings also raise concerns about the transparency of food labeling and the responsibility of manufacturers to provide safer options. While convenience and taste are key factors in sandwich popularity, nutritionists stress that awareness and moderation are essential. This study highlights the importance of public education regarding dietary sodium and the potential long-term health effects of frequent consumption of high-salt foods.

item.title: CERN Releases Over 17,000 KiCad Component Libraries Under Open Hardware License
item.description: CERN, a long-time supporter of open-source initiatives, has officially released its internal KiCad component libraries for public use. KiCad, pronounced 'KEE-kad', is a widely used open-source Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tool that allows both hobbyists and professional engineers to design electronic circuits and printed circuit boards (PCBs). With the popularity of KiCad, many users can now submit design files directly to electronics fabricators without converting them into traditional Gerber files. Over the years, CERN developed its own libraries of symbols and footprints to support its internal projects. These libraries contain over 17,000 components and have now been made available under the CERN Open Hardware License, ensuring that they are freely accessible and can be used, modified, and shared by anyone. This release represents a significant contribution to the electronics design community and aligns with CERN’s long-standing philosophy of promoting open-source solutions f

item.title: Early Career Portraits of Bettie Page in 1952
item.description: Bettie Mae Page (1923–2008) was a prominent American model in the 1950s, widely recognized as the “Queen of Pinups.” Known for her signature jet-black hair, blue eyes, and thick bangs, she influenced artists and popular culture for generations. Page moved to New York in 1947 and developed her iconic look by 1950, aided by photographer Jerry Tibbs, who suggested her distinctive bangs. She became active in New York City camera clubs, where amateur photographers paid to photograph models. These sessions produced thousands of images, many sold as collector sets in men’s magazines. By 1952, she began working with Irving Klaw, specializing in pinup and provocative photography that contributed to her underground fame. Page’s style combined girl-next-door charm with bold sexuality, bridging the gap between classic 1940s pinups and the more explicit imagery of the late 1950s. Her skill in posing for color film, which required precise lighting, made her highly sought after by photographers. Thou

item.title: Rare Humorous Tintype of a Union Officer Thumbing His Nose During the American Civil War, ca. 1863
item.description:
A rare and charming quarter-plate tintype from around 1863 captures an unidentified Union officer during the American Civil War playfully thumbing his nose at the camera. Unlike the typical stiff and serious military portraits of the era, this image stands out for its humor and spontaneity. Photography in the 1860s demanded long exposure times, requiring subjects to remain completely still, which made candid or silly expressions extremely difficult to capture. This makes the photo a true rarity that offers a glimpse into the lighter moments experienced by soldiers.
During downtime between battles or while encamped, soldiers often visited nearby photography studios or traveling photographers to have portraits taken to send home to loved ones. This unknown officer chose to break from convention and express personality instead of adopting the usual stoic pose. The image humanizes the individuals who lived through one of America's most devastating conflicts, showing that even amid hardsh

item.title: Trump Arrives in China Without Personal Welcome from Xi Jinping
item.description: President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing, China, on May 13, 2026, for a highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Observers noted that Xi did not personally greet Trump at the airport, an absence highlighted by progressive advocacy groups and Trump's own niece, Mary L. Trump, who described it as a deliberate diplomatic snub. Instead of a traditional presidential welcome, Trump was met by US Ambassador to China David Perdue, China’s Vice President Han Zheng, Ambassador Xie Feng, and Executive Vice Minister of Foreign Affairs Ma Zhaoxu. Analysts have interpreted this as a potentially weakening sign for Trump, especially in light of his recent inability to secure a peace deal with Iran, which China brokered independently. This development adds a layer of complexity to the U.S.-China summit, emphasizing the delicate balance of international diplomacy and signaling that Trump may face challenges in asserting influence during high-stakes negotiations. Critics argue that t

item.title: How the AI Boom Is Straining Family Life
item.description: The AI boom is not only reshaping industries but also straining family dynamics, particularly in households where men are heavily involved in AI work. Many women—dubbed 'sad wives of AI'—report feeling isolated and overwhelmed as their partners devote excessive time and emotional energy to AI projects. This obsession often leads to long hours, constant availability, and an unrelenting focus on technological advancements, leaving their spouses to manage childcare, household responsibilities, and emotional labor alone. Experts note that this phenomenon reflects historical patterns seen during prior technological booms, where one partner becomes consumed by the work while the other bears the domestic burden. Women are also less likely to use generative AI at work, compounding disparities in access to AI's benefits. Some spouses turn to AI tools like ChatGPT for emotional support, but these solutions can validate negative feelings rather than resolve conflicts. While a few families find AI

item.title: Thinking Machines Develops AI That Can Process and Respond Simultaneously
item.description: Thinking Machines Lab, founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, has introduced a new type of AI called 'interaction models,' designed to mimic real human conversation. Unlike traditional AI models that operate sequentially—where the user speaks and the AI responds—these models can process input and generate a response simultaneously, a capability known as 'full duplex.' The startup claims its model, TML-Interaction-Small, can respond in just 0.40 seconds, a speed comparable to natural human dialogue and faster than current models from OpenAI and Google. While these developments are promising, the AI is currently only available in a limited research preview, with a broader public release planned later this year. The innovation represents a significant shift in AI interactivity, aiming to make communication with machines more fluid and conversational. However, experts note that the real-world effectiveness of the technology will only be verifiable once users can test it extensively. Thi

item.title: Pope Appoints Pastor Critical of Trump Policy to Lead Conservative Florida Diocese
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has appointed Father Emilio Biosca Agüero, a pastor in Washington D.C. known for criticizing the Trump administration's immigration enforcement policies, as the new Bishop of Naples, Florida. The Diocese of Naples encompasses a large portion of southwest Florida, including Fort Myers, Sarasota, and surrounding counties, which are traditionally Republican strongholds with a growing Hispanic Catholic population. Father Biosca has previously expressed frustration that ICE raids disrupted his congregation, leading to deportations and declining attendance at his parish, the Shrine of the Sacred Heart, where most members are from El Salvador. This appointment follows a pattern of Pope Leo XIV placing clergy with Central American ties in key positions overseeing areas that voted strongly for Trump. Earlier appointments include Rev. Manuel de Jesús Rodríguez in Palm Beach and Evelio Menjivar-Ayala in West Virginia. These moves reflect the Vatican's increasing attention to immigrat

item.title: Exploring Time and Transformation Through David Ambarzumjan’s Brushstrokes
item.description: David Ambarzumjan’s 'Brushstrokes in Time' series explores the intersection of history, nature, and human influence through a unique approach to painting. Each piece begins with a recognizable scene—a rainy city street, a desert at dusk, or a sunlit cobblestone bridge—and is disrupted by a bold, sculptural stroke of oil paint that seems to tear the canvas open. This stroke reveals another era or dimension, creating a dialogue between past and present. The artist’s focus is not merely on contrasting old and new but on understanding the connections between them and the traces left by human and natural transformations. His work resides in the space between realistic depiction and imaginative interpretation, using oil paint to achieve effects that photography cannot. By juxtaposing tangible scenes with surreal ruptures, Ambarzumjan invites viewers to reflect on time, memory, and the lasting impact of human activity on the natural world. His work has been exhibited online and on social medi

item.title: The Dachau Bell: A Symbol of Hope and Freedom in 1945
item.description: During the final days of World War II, prisoners at Dachau concentration camp created a small bell from scrap metal and wire, which became a poignant symbol of hope and resistance amid the camp's extreme suffering. The bell was quietly rung to mark personal milestones such as birthdays, the New Year, or the passing of fellow prisoners, offering a fleeting sense of humanity in an environment dominated by fear and oppression. On April 29, 1945, as American forces liberated Dachau, the bell rang openly for the first time, signifying not only freedom from captivity but the reclamation of dignity and the resilience of the human spirit. After the liberation, prisoners buried the bell in the camp yard as a way to honor their suffering and preserve the memory of their experiences. Decades later, the bell was rediscovered, rusted but intact, standing as a testament to endurance, survival, and the enduring capacity for hope in the face of unimaginable hardship. The story of the bell continues to

item.title: A Retrospective Look at Sandra Dee’s Career and Iconic Roles in the 1960s
item.description: Sandra Dee, born Alexandra Zuck in 1942, was an American actress renowned for her portrayal of the quintessential 'girl-next-door' ingénue. Starting as a child model, she transitioned to film in her teens and gained fame with performances in Until They Sail (1957), Imitation of Life, Gidget, and A Summer Place, all in 1959. These roles established her as a top-tier box office star entering the 1960s. Dee took over the role of Tammy Tyree from Debbie Reynolds, starring in Tammy Tell Me True (1961) and Tammy and the Doctor (1963). Her popularity peaked in 1961, ranking her sixth among the most popular money-making stars in the U.S. Her wholesome image became iconic, later parodied in the musical Grease. In 1960, Dee met pop singer Bobby Darin during the filming of Come September, and the two married the same year, becoming one of Hollywood’s most photographed couples. They co-starred in three films, further capitalizing on their real-life romance. As Hollywood shifted towards the grittie

item.title: Shirley Temple: From Child Star to Diplomat and Cultural Icon
item.description: Shirley Temple (1928–2014) was one of Hollywood’s most celebrated child stars and later became a pioneering diplomat. Known for her 56 signature curls, Shirley’s mother meticulously styled her hair for every film to maintain a consistent image. Before her famous Fox movies, she appeared in the controversial 'Baby Burlesks' shorts, where toddlers parodied adult films. Shirley was almost cast as Dorothy in 'The Wizard of Oz,' but the role eventually went to Judy Garland. She became a box-office sensation during the Great Depression, saving 20th Century Fox from financial collapse, and was the top-grossing star worldwide from 1935 to 1938. Due to her fame, the FBI monitored her, and a bodyguard was hired to protect her from kidnapping threats. To maintain her youthful image, her studio even altered her birth certificate. Shirley made history on-screen with Bill Robinson as the first interracial dance duo in 'The Little Colonel.' Despite the popular mocktail bearing her name, she disliked

item.title: White House Aides Report Friction Over Natalie Harp's Role in Managing Trump's Late-Night Truth Social Posts
item.description: According to a Wall Street Journal analysis, President Donald Trump has posted more than 8,800 times on Truth Social since returning to the White House in January 2025. This includes dozens of late-night posting bursts between 8 p.m. and 6 a.m., with one night featuring 55 posts after a full day of official duties. These posts often include conspiracy theories, personal attacks, and recycled content from other social media sources. Natalie Harp, Trump's executive assistant, plays a key role by preparing printed drafts of posts for his approval and then publishing them in batches, sometimes at odd hours. She reportedly bypasses standard White House review processes, answering only to Trump directly, which has caused internal tensions with other staff including the chief of staff's office and communications team. Other aides have nicknamed her the 'human printer' for carrying stacks of material. White House officials confirm Trump approves all content, though he posts some himself. The a

item.title: Quintessenz Revitalizes a 400-Year-Old Greek Ruin with Colorful Tapestries
item.description: In the small town of Kagatika on Paxos island, Greece, a 400-year-old ruin that once served as a factory has been transformed by an artistic installation created by the design duo Quintessenz. The installation features a series of colorful tapestries spanning the entire spectrum of the rainbow. The movement of the wind animates the fabrics, giving them a dynamic, almost digital-like presence. This interplay of natural light and kinetic motion adds a captivating layer to the historic structure, creating a poetic contrast between contemporary art and centuries-old architecture. The installation has drawn attention for its aesthetic appeal and innovative approach, breathing new life into a site that might otherwise remain overlooked. It demonstrates how art can interact with historical spaces to create immersive, visually stimulating experiences for viewers. The project reflects a growing trend of using art to engage with heritage sites, highlighting the importance of creative interventio

item.title: Trump Faces Backlash from MAGA Supporters Over Remarks on Americans' Finances
item.description: President Donald Trump recently made a statement admitting that Americans' financial situations do not influence his decision-making, sparking unusual criticism from within his own MAGA support base. When asked whether the financial concerns of Americans motivate his deals, Trump responded, 'Not even a little bit…I don't think about Americans' financial situations.' This candid acknowledgment contrasts sharply with his 2024 campaign promises, which emphasized prioritizing working-class Americans and protecting their economic interests. Following the remark, MAGA influencers and activists expressed disapproval on social media. Eric Spracklen called it a 'disgusting betrayal,' stating that Trump no longer represents the man he voted for. Conservative commentator Auron MacIntyre suggested that while Trump might be prioritizing strategic considerations like Iran's economic influence, the statement alienates supporters already facing financial strain. Other far-right figures and January 6 a

item.title: U.S. Army Faces Budget Shortfalls and Training Cuts Amid Expanding Missions
item.description: The U.S. Army is confronting a significant budget shortfall estimated at up to $6 billion due to expanded missions both domestically and abroad under President Donald Trump's administration. The financial strain has forced the Army to reduce training programs, leading to the cancellation of key courses at elite military schools and at the unit level months before the fiscal year ends. Major cost drivers include the ongoing conflict in Iran, increased border security operations along the southern U.S. border, and extensive National Guard deployments, including a mission in Washington, D.C., projected to cost around $1.1 billion this year. Additionally, the Army is managing rising personnel costs and covering funding gaps from a 76-day Department of Homeland Security shutdown. The III Armored Corps, which commands nearly half of the Army's combat power, is particularly affected, with aviation units expected to deploy at reduced readiness and mid-level officer careers potentially stagnati

item.title: Rising Water Costs in the U.S. Linked to Climate Change and Droughts
item.description: Across the United States, water systems are facing unprecedented stress due to climate change, which is contributing to prolonged droughts and more intense storms. Cities like Corpus Christi, Texas, have seen their reservoirs fall to just a tenth of capacity, raising the real risk of running out of water within months. Utilities are struggling to maintain supply while costs rise, with water and related services increasing at nearly twice the rate of inflation. Experts warn that these trends are not isolated; communities nationwide may experience similar crises as climate patterns continue to intensify. The rising expenses are affecting households directly, with water bills climbing sharply as municipalities invest in infrastructure, conservation measures, and emergency responses. The situation underscores the urgent need for long-term planning, climate adaptation strategies, and sustainable water management to prevent widespread shortages and financial strain for residents.

item.title: Scientists Detect Early Signs of Tectonic Activity in Zambia's Kafue Rift
item.description: Researchers have identified early indicators of tectonic rifting beneath the Kafue Rift in Zambia, suggesting the potential formation of a new tectonic boundary in central Africa. By analyzing helium isotopes in geothermal spring waters, scientists discovered signatures that point to a direct connection with Earth's mantle, located between 25 and 100 miles below the surface. This evidence supports the idea that the Kafue Rift—and the broader Southwest African Rift Zone—may be experiencing active tectonic activity. Such rifting is part of Earth's long-term geological processes, which shape continents, recycle minerals, and drive volcanic and geothermal activity. While the African continent is already known for tectonic activity along the East African Rift and the Afar Depression, the findings in Zambia represent a previously unconfirmed site of early rifting.
