Beyond her filmography, Torres is remembered as a symbol of early Hollywood’s charm and innovation. Her legacy continues to inspire interest in the history of cinema and the contributions of Latina talent during the golden age of film. Vintage photographs

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Portraits of Audrey Hepburn Filming War and Peace (1956)
item.description: In 1956, Audrey Hepburn took on the iconic role of Natasha Rostova in the epic adaptation of Tolstoy’s 'War and Peace,' directed by King Vidor. These 40 vintage portraits showcase Hepburn both in character and candid moments on set, highlighting her timeless elegance and the youthful energy she brought to the role. The photographs capture her in period-accurate costumes, including high-waisted Empire dresses and intricate hairstyles of the Napoleonic era, reflecting the film’s meticulous attention to historical detail. Away from the grandeur of the ballrooms and battlefield scenes, these behind-the-scenes images reveal Hepburn’s natural charm, poise, and dedication to her craft. They serve not only as a testament to her enduring legacy as a cinematic icon but also as a visual documentation of one of the most ambitious film productions of the 1950s. Her portrayal of Natasha Rostova remains celebrated for its grace, vulnerability, and depth, cementing her status as the perfect embodiment

item.title: Understanding the Hantavirus Outbreak on a Cruise Ship
item.description: More than 100 passengers on the MV Hondius cruise ship were recently evacuated due to a hantavirus outbreak, with 18 Americans placed in biocontainment units in Nebraska and Georgia. As of now, 11 cases have been reported, including three deaths, all among passengers or crew. The virus identified is the Andes virus, a rare type of hantavirus capable of person-to-person transmission, although it spreads primarily through exposure to contaminated rodent droppings or urine. Infectious disease expert Nicole Iovine explains that the Andes virus infects deep in the lungs, making it far less contagious than COVID-19, which infects both the upper and lower respiratory tracts. Symptoms can appear between five days to six weeks after exposure and start with flu-like signs before potentially developing into severe disease. Close, prolonged contact is generally required for transmission, and casual encounters, like sitting next to someone at a dining table, carry minimal risk. Good ventilation and

item.title: Epstein Survivor Criticizes DOJ for Failing to Protect Her Identity in Released Files
item.description: During a House Oversight Committee hearing, an Epstein survivor identified as 'Roza' spoke out against the U.S. Department of Justice for failing to protect her anonymity in the release of files related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Roza, whose name appeared over 500 times in the documents, expressed that powerful figures were redacted while her identity was exposed, leaving her feeling unsafe. She recounted her first encounter with French modeling scout Jean-Luc Brunel at age 18, who recruited her into modeling and facilitated her U.S. immigration, a process financed by Epstein. Roza described how the sexual abuse began while Epstein was serving an 18-month sentence for a previous child abuse conviction, with at least 40 minor victims identified by the FBI. She criticized the DOJ’s actions as deliberate, stating that revealing her name while protecting other influential individuals was a conscious choice. Representative Robert García labeled her testimony a 'horrific exam

item.title: Rep. Norma Torres Presses HUD Secretary Scott Turner on California Fire Relief Funding
item.description: During a House Appropriations subcommittee hearing on May 12, 2026, Rep. Norma Torres (D-CA) confronted Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Scott Turner over delays in disaster relief funding for Californians affected by the January 2025 Southern California fires. Torres repeatedly asked when the Trump administration would submit a disaster package, emphasizing the urgent needs of families whose homes and lives were devastated. Turner mentioned visiting affected communities but deflected direct answers, citing local leadership and prior funding issues. Torres stressed that California taxpayers contribute significantly more in federal taxes than they receive in federal funding and demanded a government response that prioritizes the state’s recovery needs. The exchange drew attention for Torres’ persistent and direct questioning, highlighting tensions over federal disaster relief and the Trump administration’s fiscal priorities. The interaction also touched on broader concerns, su

item.title: Michael and La Toya Jackson in New York: Rare Candid Photos from 1977
item.description: In 1977, Michael Jackson moved to New York City to film the musical movie The Wiz, in which he played the Scarecrow. During this time, he shared a Manhattan apartment with his sister La Toya Jackson. Photographer Araldo di Crollalanza captured rare candid moments of the siblings, showing them in their daily lives — relaxing in their living space, dining together, and spending time in the kitchen. Beyond their home life, Michael and La Toya frequently attended high-profile events, including a Woody Allen party in August and a press conference for The Wiz in September. They also became regular visitors at the legendary nightclub Studio 54, where Michael celebrated his 19th birthday with La Toya and their sister Janet. Author Truman Capote famously described the Jackson siblings as “oases of innocence” amid the hedonistic New York nightlife. These photos offer a rare glimpse into their lives during a pivotal year that marked Michael’s first major film project and his initial collaboration

item.title: Trump Admits He Does Not Consider Americans' Financial Situations in Iran War Decisions
item.description: During a press interaction on May 12, 2026, former President Donald Trump made a statement indicating that Americans' financial situations do not influence his decision-making regarding the Iran war. When asked by a reporter about whether the financial impact on Americans motivates him to negotiate an end to the conflict, Trump replied, 'Not even a little bit. I don't think about Americans' financial situation.' This comment sparked immediate backlash on social media, with multiple Democratic politicians and progressive outlets highlighting the remark as evidence of Trump's disregard for ordinary citizens' economic hardships. Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro referenced high gas prices, while Rep. Adriano Espaillat and Rep. Katherine Clark criticized Trump for ignoring the rising costs of living, healthcare, and groceries. Political commentators and podcast hosts described the comment as potentially damaging for Trump's political image, suggesting it could be widely used in Democratic

item.title: New Jersey Republican Congressman Remains Absent Due to Unspecified Illness
item.description: New Jersey Republican Congressman Tom Kean Jr. has been absent from Capitol Hill for over two months due to an unspecified medical condition. Kean has not voted since March 5, and his office has only described his situation as a 'personal medical issue,' promising a full recovery without providing further details. This extended absence has raised questions from both political commentators and the public about transparency and accountability, especially given reports that stock trades continue to be executed on his behalf during his recuperation. The Democratic House Majority PAC has highlighted his absence in a satirical campaign referencing the popular 'Where in the World is Carmen Sandiego?' video game series. Political commentators have criticized the lack of communication regarding his situation, emphasizing that such a prolonged absence would be unacceptable in most professional settings. Kean’s continued absence and refusal to provide additional information keep the public and po

item.title: Hacks Creators Discuss AI, Media, and Gender in Entertainment
item.description: Ahead of the finale of HBO Max's hit show Hacks, cocreators Paul W. Downs and Lucia Aniello discussed the challenges and themes explored in the series, emphasizing the importance of human creativity and the risks posed by AI. The show, praised for its sharp writing and the dynamic relationship between its central female characters, also serves as a commentary on broader entertainment industry issues, including media consolidation, censorship, and gender dynamics. Downs and Aniello highlighted how women in comedy often face unique challenges and limited opportunities compared to men, drawing on both real-life experiences and the narrative arcs of characters Deborah Vance and Ava Daniels. They also touched on the risks of corporate control over content, noting how decisions by a few executives can affect a show's availability and creative freedom. The creators expressed strong opposition to the use of AI in creative processes, citing concerns about undermining talent and employment oppor

item.title: Trump confronts reporter questions on Iran war and impact on Americans
item.description: During a press briefing on Tuesday, former President Donald Trump lost his composure when reporters questioned him about the impact of his military actions in Iran on ordinary Americans. After emphasizing that preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon was his top priority, Trump was asked about rising pressures on Americans and economic concerns. He responded sharply, asking, 'Are you listening to me?' and cited a poll indicating that 85 percent of Americans understood the importance of preventing Iran from obtaining nuclear capabilities. Earlier in the briefing, Trump had remarked that he did not consider Americans' financial situations when making decisions. The session became chaotic when an individual shouted, 'Stop the bloodshed!' although it was unclear whether the person was a reporter or a protester. The episode highlights tensions between the former president and the media over the consequences of his foreign policy decisions, especially as questions about domestic impac

item.title: Discovery of Bicharracosaurus Dionidei Offers New Insights into Jurassic Sauropods
item.description: Paleontologists in Argentina have uncovered a new species of giant dinosaur, named Bicharracosaurus dionidei, providing fresh insights into Jurassic-era sauropods in the Southern Hemisphere. Measuring approximately 65 feet (20 meters) in length, this dinosaur exhibited a unique combination of features from both Diplodocus and Brachiosaurus relatives. Fossils recovered from the Cañadón Calcáreo Formation in Patagonia include over 30 vertebrae, ribs, and part of the pelvis, indicating an adult specimen that lived around 155 million years ago. Phylogenetic analyses suggest Bicharracosaurus dionidei belongs to the Brachiosauridae family, making it the first known Jurassic brachiosaurid from South America. This discovery fills a significant gap in the fossil record and helps scientists better understand the evolution of giant sauropods across Gondwana. The species was named in honor of local shepherd Dionide Mesa, who discovered the first fossils. The genus name 'Bicharracosaurus' comes fro

item.title: Princess Nirgidma of Torghut: Life, Legacy, and Cultural Heritage
item.description: Princess Nirgidma of Torghut, born in 1907 in Tokyo, was a distinguished noblewoman whose life bridged traditional Mongolian nomadic culture and the cosmopolitan world of 20th-century Europe and Asia. Daughter of Prince Palta, a Mongolian statesman, and Orloma, a Torghut noblewoman, Nirgidma inherited a rich lineage tracing back to Genghis Khan. She received an extensive education, attending Sacred Heart School in Beijing and universities in Paris and Brussels, becoming fluent in multiple languages including French, English, Chinese, Russian, Mongolian, Persian, and Arabic. In 1932, photographer Maynard Owen Williams captured her with her hunting eagle for National Geographic, symbolizing her blend of nobility and cultural pride. She contributed to literature and scholarship, publishing 'Dix-Huit Chants et Poèmes Mongols' in 1937 and writing articles reflecting on gender, culture, and tradition. Nirgidma married French diplomat Michel Georges Eugène Bréal in 1938, and they had one son.

item.title: Milly Vitale: Celebrating the Elegance and Legacy of a Post-War Italian Star
item.description: Milly Vitale (1933–2006) was a celebrated Italian actress whose charm and talent defined a golden era of post-war Italian cinema. Known for her delicate beauty and expressive presence, Vitale emerged during the Hollywood-on-the-Tiber period and successfully crossed over into international productions. She is widely remembered for her performance in the 1955 musical 'The Seven Little Foys' alongside Bob Hope, showcasing her ability to convey both lighthearted and dramatic roles. Beyond her screen performances, Vitale embodied the elegance and resilience of 1950s womanhood, becoming an enduring symbol of Italian cinematic grace. Although she chose to retire early from acting to focus on her personal life, her influence continues to resonate among vintage cinema enthusiasts. Photographs and portraits of Vitale capture the timeless allure that made her a standout figure of her generation. Today, her contributions to Italian and international cinema remain celebrated, preserving her status

item.title: The Rise of At-Home Vaginal Microbiome Testing and Its Implications
item.description: A growing number of women are using at-home vaginal microbiome tests to monitor and improve their vaginal health, often motivated by recurring infections, fertility concerns, or curiosity. Startups like TinyHealth, Evvy, Neueve, and Juno Bio offer kits that provide insight into the balance of 'good' and 'bad' bacteria in the vagina. The tests have gained widespread attention after Silicon Valley entrepreneur Bryan Johnson publicly highlighted his girlfriend's high microbiome score. While some women report relief from conditions like aerobic vaginitis and bacterial vaginosis after following test recommendations, experts caution that the vaginal microbiome is highly dynamic and influenced by factors like diet, sexual activity, menstruation, and race. There is limited research validating the long-term accuracy of these tests, and no at-home kits are FDA-approved. Despite potential benefits, frequent testing can induce anxiety and lead to unnecessary treatments that may disrupt the natural

item.title: Fashion Portraits of Pattie Boyd Highlighting 1960s London Style
item.description: Pattie Boyd emerged as one of the most iconic faces of British fashion during the Swinging Sixties. With her long golden hair, wide eyes, and effortless elegance, she embodied the era's ideals of beauty and youthful rebellion. Boyd's modeling career flourished as she appeared in a variety of settings, from Covent Garden markets to high-fashion photo sessions, captured by renowned photographers such as Eugene Vernier, Larry Ellis, John French, David Bailey, and Norman Parkinson. She showcased a wide range of styles, including mod outfits, bohemian dresses, and glamorous evening wear, reflecting the vibrant and experimental fashion scene of 1960s London. Boyd became a muse for prominent designers like Ossie Clark and appeared in campaigns for major fashion publications including Vogue UK and Harper's Bazaar. Her images convey both innocence and seduction, establishing her as a timeless style icon. These portraits not only document Boyd's beauty and charisma but also offer a visual snapsh

item.title: Exploring Psilocybin as a Potential Treatment for OCD
item.description: Adam Strauss, a successful digital entrepreneur and trained musician, struggled with severe obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD), which made even minor decisions, like choosing an MP3 player, paralyzing. OCD, characterized by obsessive thoughts and compulsive behaviors, is driven by deep discomfort with uncertainty and heightened self-doubt. Conventional treatments such as cognitive behavioral therapy and antidepressants often had limited success, leaving many patients like Adam searching for alternatives. Adam discovered a 2006 clinical study showing that psilocybin, the active compound in magic mushrooms, could alleviate OCD symptoms by disrupting habitual neural patterns and promoting openness. Driven by desperation, Adam began experimenting with synthetic psychedelics in controlled settings, documenting his experiences and adjusting variables such as dosage and environment to maximize the potential effect. Psychedelics can help the brain form new connections and break entrenched cyc

item.title: White House Officials Express Frustration with Trump's Leadership in Undercover Footage
item.description: Undercover footage released by right-wing journalist James O'Keefe shows several White House staffers expressing frustration with President Donald Trump's management style. Benjamin Elliston, a senior budget analyst, told an undercover reporter that Trump is creating chaos, particularly criticizing a proposed ballroom project with a secret military basement, and expressed concerns over the unclear funding sources. Maxim Lott, a special assistant on domestic policy, described how lower-ranking staff often make decisions for the president without formal cost-benefit analysis, highlighting what he perceives as an uncontrolled work environment. The footage comes as Republicans in Congress consider allocating $1 billion in taxpayer funds toward the ballroom project. Raw Story notes it has not independently verified the unedited video, and O'Keefe has a history of releasing selectively edited content. The revelations provide a glimpse into internal dissatisfaction among staffers, though the

item.title: Trump Names Acting FDA Director After Marty Makary Departs Amid Controversy
item.description: President Donald Trump announced a leadership change at the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on May 12, 2026, appointing Kyle Diamantas as acting commissioner following the departure of FDA Commissioner Marty Makary. Trump publicly praised Makary's accomplishments on his Truth Social platform, describing him as a hardworking and respected figure who had achieved significant progress during his tenure. However, reports indicate that Trump had recently approved a plan to remove Makary, reflecting internal pressures and disagreements within the administration. Makary faced criticism for resisting the approval of flavored e-cigarettes and for delays in the safety review of mifepristone, a key medication used in abortion procedures. His exit follows the departures of several other high-profile Trump administration officials, including Attorney General Pam Bondi, Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, and Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer. Kyle Diamantas, previously the FDA's Deputy Co

item.title: SEC Coaches Criticize Ole Miss Amid Recruiting and Academic Controversies
item.description: Ole Miss continues to draw criticism from coaches across the SEC following recent comments and controversies surrounding its football program. Lane Kiffin, now LSU's head coach, faced backlash for remarks regarding the challenges of recruiting players to Oxford, Mississippi, suggesting that family and historical context influenced athletes' decisions. Kiffin later clarified that his comments were factual, not intended to offend, but the remarks still drew significant media attention. Shortly after, Texas head coach Steve Sarkisian added to the scrutiny, criticizing the academic rigor at Ole Miss by jokingly referencing 'basketweaving' classes as a path to a degree. Sarkisian also highlighted broader concerns about NCAA rules and the transfer portal, noting inconsistencies in how student-athletes' academic credits are handled across programs. Ole Miss athletic director Keith Carter responded, emphasizing that the attention reflects discomfort some have with the program's success. The co

item.title: Alice Faye: Iconic Portraits of the Hollywood Star in the 1930s
item.description: Alice Faye, born Alice Jeanne Leppert on May 5, 1915, was a major Hollywood musical star and singer known as the 'Queen of Fox' at 20th Century-Fox during the 1930s. She transitioned from Broadway chorus girl and radio performer to one of the era’s top box-office stars, appearing in films such as George White’s Scandals (1934), On the Avenue (1937), Alexander’s Ragtime Band (1938), and In Old Chicago (1938). Her career breakthrough came in 1934 when she replaced Lillian Harvey in George White’s Scandals, performing alongside Rudy Vallée. Under the mentorship of studio head Darryl F. Zanuck, Faye’s image evolved from a brassy, platinum blonde showgirl to a softer, wholesome, and glamorous 'girl next door' ideal, perfect for Technicolor musicals and romantic roles. Known for her warm contralto singing voice, charming personality, and consistent box-office success, she introduced many hit songs and became a top-10 draw by the late 1930s. Early Hollywood styling gave her a platinum look wi

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel and Senator Chris Van Hollen clash over drinking allegations during Senate hearing
item.description: During a tense Senate Appropriations Committee hearing on May 12, 2026, FBI Director Kash Patel clashed with Senator Chris Van Hollen over allegations of excessive drinking and unexplained absences by Patel. Van Hollen cited multiple reports, including one from The Atlantic, and asked Patel to testify on whether these accusations were categorically false. Patel denied all allegations, describing them as 'unequivocally false' and asserting he maintains constant communication with law enforcement officials. The exchange escalated when Patel fired back with unverified personal attacks against Van Hollen, accusing the senator of drinking on taxpayer-funded trips and running up a bar tab—claims Van Hollen denied and which have no independent verification. The confrontation drew significant attention for its heated nature, highlighting partisan tensions and raising questions about Patel's conduct as FBI Director. This incident underscores the ongoing scrutiny of high-ranking officials in sen

item.title: Senator John Kennedy Denies Making Statement Attributed to Him by Trump
item.description: U.S. Senator John Neely Kennedy of Louisiana publicly refuted a statement that former President Donald Trump attributed to him in a recent Truth Social post. Trump claimed that Kennedy was demanding former President Barack Obama return $120 million allegedly earned through Obamacare-related ownership. The fabricated quote also suggested unethical behavior by Obama. Kennedy clarified to NOTUS that he never made such a statement and expressed uncertainty about the origin of the false claim circulating online. In the same Truth Social post, Trump repeated a previously debunked claim that election systems across the U.S. deleted millions of votes cast for him. The White House defended Trump's remarks, emphasizing the administration's commitment to election integrity. This incident highlights ongoing political tensions and the spread of misinformation among high-profile political figures. Kennedy's explicit denial serves as a corrective to public confusion caused by the misleading post.

item.title: Charles Verschuuren Jr., Dutch-American Illustrator and Portrait Painter
item.description: Charles Verschuuren Jr. (1891–1955) was a talented Dutch-American illustrator and painter who successfully navigated both commercial art and fine portraiture. Born in the Netherlands into an artistic family, he brought a refined European influence to the American art scene, particularly in New York. His work is especially noted for vibrant, dynamic posters and advertisements from the 1910s and 1920s that captured the era's aesthetic with bold colors and strong compositional skills. These commercial pieces promoted various products, events, and theaters, including posters for shoe creams like Erdal, cabarets, operettas, exhibitions, and festivals in the Netherlands. Beyond advertising, Verschuuren excelled as a portraitist, known for his graceful ability to convey the dignity and personality of his subjects. His career exemplifies the early 20th-century convergence of art and industry, where illustrators played key roles in visual communication and branding. The collection of works show

item.title: Study Finds Half of Caribbean Reefs with Highest Coastal Protection Value Remain Unprotected
item.description: Coral reefs serve as vital natural barriers against hurricanes and storm surges in the Caribbean, reducing wave energy by up to 97% and preventing billions of dollars in annual storm damage globally. A new study highlights the growing threats these ecosystems face from rising ocean temperatures, pollution, and coastal development, which are causing coral loss and weakening their protective structures. Researchers analyzed which reefs are most likely to persist under future warming scenarios and which provide the greatest coastal protection based on the types of coral species present. Reefs built by large, complex corals like elkhorn offer superior wave-breaking capabilities compared to those with smaller or flatter species. The analysis reveals that approximately half of the highest-priority reefs — those combining strong protective function with better resilience to warming — currently lack formal protection from human impacts. This is particularly evident in areas like the Bahamas, P

item.title: NASA Landsat Interactive Uses Satellite Imagery to Spell Names with Earth’s Landscapes
item.description:
NASA, in collaboration with the U.S. Geological Survey, has introduced a creative Earth Day 2026 project called “Your Name in Landsat,” which transforms satellite imagery into a playful, personalized experience. The interactive tool allows users to type in any word or name and generates a vertical sequence of satellite image tiles from the Landsat archive that resemble letters of the alphabet. These letters are not artificially designed but are instead found within natural and human-made landscapes captured from space over decades.
The Landsat program, which has been observing Earth continuously since 1972, provides one of the longest-running records of the planet’s surface. Its imagery is widely used by scientists to study environmental changes such as deforestation, urban expansion, agricultural patterns, wildfire impacts, water use, and coastal erosion. In this new interactive format, that vast scientific archive is reimagined as a visual alphabet, turning global geography into a f

item.title: Affordable Nail Strengthener Review: Modelones Milky Nail Concealer for Brittle Nails
item.description: If you struggle with thin, brittle nails or damage from acrylics and gel manicures, the Modelones Milky Nail Concealer may be a budget-friendly solution. Priced under $10 on Amazon, this 3-in-1 product acts as a nail strengthener, base coat, and glossy top coat. Unlike traditional clear nail treatments, it provides a subtle pearly white color that can be worn alone or under nail polish. Formulated with keratin for strengthening, jojoba oil for repair, and evening primrose oil for hydration, users report healthier, stronger nails without harsh chemical smells. Reviewers praise its smooth application, quick drying time, and lasting effect, noting that it can even support nail growth for those with naturally fragile nails. Many say it outperforms higher-priced alternatives like Londontown’s Kur nail concealer. The product is especially appreciated by those who want minimal preparation, a natural-looking finish, and durability through everyday activities such as washing dishes, typing, or

item.title: Michelle Pfeiffer's Early Career: From Beauty Pageants to Hollywood
item.description: Before becoming an Academy Award-nominated actress and Hollywood icon, Michelle Pfeiffer participated in beauty pageants as a stepping stone to her acting career. In 1978, at 19 years old, she won the Miss Orange County pageant and went on to compete in the Miss California contest held at Magic Mountain in Valencia. While she did not win the overall title, she finished sixth and earned the Miss Photogenic award. Pfeiffer’s strategic participation in these pageants allowed her to attract the attention of a Hollywood agent, leading to her first agent and her television debut on Fantasy Island later that year. At the time, she was a student at Golden West College and worked as a check-out clerk at Vons supermarket. Interestingly, the 1954 Miss California winner, Lee Meriwether, also went on to portray Catwoman, a role Pfeiffer would famously redefine decades later in Batman Returns (1992). Reflecting on her pageant days, Pfeiffer has spoken with humor and humility, acknowledging that she

item.title: Why Suspending the Federal Gas Tax Would Only Slightly Reduce Gas Prices
item.description: With gasoline prices averaging over $4.50 per gallon in mid-May 2026, President Donald Trump proposed suspending the federal gas tax, which is 18.4 cents per gallon for gasoline and 24.3 cents for diesel. While some states have temporarily suspended their own fuel taxes, research suggests that drivers typically receive only about 79% of the intended tax reduction. Overall, a gas tax suspension would likely lower national gas prices by only around 4%, and even less in high-cost states like California. Gasoline prices are influenced primarily by crude oil, which accounts for roughly half of the pump price, with refining, distribution, marketing, and taxes making up the rest. Supply shocks, such as disruptions from the Iran war in 2026, can push prices higher because drivers cannot quickly reduce consumption. California faces additional price pressures from stricter environmental regulations, geographical isolation, and limited refinery competition. Distribution and marketing costs, inclu

item.title: Texas Tech Law Student Denied Preliminary Injunction in Case Over Comments About Charlie Kirk
item.description: A Trump-appointed federal judge in North Texas has denied a preliminary injunction requested by Texas Tech law student Ellie Mae Fisher. Fisher had been investigated by Texas Tech, a public university, for making celebratory remarks following the assassination of right-wing activist Charlie Kirk at a political event in Utah last September. Fisher anticipated potential disciplinary action from the university and possible state bar referral, leading her to file a lawsuit citing First Amendment rights. U.S. District Judge Brantley Starr ruled that the request for a preliminary injunction could not proceed due to Texas Tech’s sovereign immunity under the 11th Amendment, which protects states and state-run institutions from certain federal lawsuits. While the ruling does not address whether Fisher’s First Amendment rights were violated or whether she could pursue damages later, the judge emphasized that the court cannot grant future injunctions to undo past actions. This case comes amid hei

item.title: Tennessee law allows judicial removal for non-cooperation with federal immigration authorities
item.description: Tennessee Governor Bill Lee has signed legislation that could lead to the removal of state judges who fail to cooperate with federal immigration authorities, including Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Sponsored by Republican legislators Rep. Rick Scarbrough and Sen. Paul Rose, the law mandates that all courts comply with requests from the U.S. Department of Homeland Security and related federal agencies. Judges who are deemed to 'obstruct lawful operations' may be referred to the state judicial oversight board for misconduct proceedings, which could ultimately result in their removal from office. The law does not explicitly define what constitutes obstructive actions, and sponsors did not specify whether there was a particular problem prompting the measure. ICE has recently conducted mass arrests in Tennessee for individuals appearing at traffic and misdemeanor court summons, raising questions about coordination with local courts. Efforts in the state Senate to soften the leg

item.title: Julianne Phillips: A Look Back at Her Modeling and Acting Career in the 1980s
item.description: Julianne Phillips, born May 6, 1960, rose to fame in the 1980s as a successful model and emerging actress, best known for her marriage to rock legend Bruce Springsteen from 1985 to 1989. With her tall stature, long blonde hair, and athletic build, she epitomized 1980s beauty and landed high-paying modeling contracts, reportedly earning up to $2,000 per day at her career peak. Phillips transitioned into acting, appearing in music videos for .38 Special and Bruce Springsteen, as well as in TV films like Summer Fantasy and His Mistress. She later took roles in feature films such as Odd Jobs, Fletch Lives, and Skin Deep. Her relationship with Springsteen attracted international media attention, and their divorce in 1989 inspired Springsteen’s 1987 album, Tunnel of Love. Following the 1980s, Phillips found notable professional success in the early 1990s as Francesca “Frankie” Reed on the TV drama Sisters. Her career trajectory showcases a blend of modeling, acting, and celebrity influence d

item.title: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary resigns amid dispute over flavored vape approvals
item.description: Marty Makary, the Commissioner of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, resigned from his position on Tuesday after ongoing tensions with President Donald Trump regarding flavored vape nicotine products. The dispute reportedly arose because Trump was frustrated with Makary's perceived slow pace in approving flavored vape products for retail. Earlier this year, the administration aimed to expand access to these products, but Makary's office issued a memo that blocked several flavors from being authorized, putting him at odds with the president. Sources indicate that Makary's position had been precarious for some time, with his tenure characterized by mass layoffs, turnover among senior officials, and frequent policy clashes with lawmakers, pharmaceutical companies, and the president himself. Makary's departure marks the fourth high-profile exit from the Trump administration this year, highlighting ongoing instability within the leadership team. The decision to resign was reportedly mon

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Photos and Stories from George Clooney’s Work on The Peacemaker (1997)
item.description:
This article presents a collection of behind-the-scenes photographs from the 1997 film The Peacemaker, offering a glimpse into George Clooney’s early years as a rising Hollywood star. At the time, Clooney was transitioning into major film roles and working on one of DreamWorks Pictures’ first high-profile releases. The images are accompanied by anecdotes that highlight his professionalism, personality, and relationships on set.
Director Mimi Leder described Clooney as “fearless,” emphasizing his willingness to engage in physically demanding scenes and stunts to enhance realism. His co-star Nicole Kidman recalled that Clooney fostered a lighthearted atmosphere during filming, helping balance the intensity of the movie’s subject matter with humor and camaraderie.
Despite his growing fame, Clooney reportedly remained humble. He was said to be starstruck by veteran actor Armin Mueller-Stahl and even offered to get him coffee. Because Clooney and Kidman had little rehearsal time, he made

item.title: Anastasia Sierra Explores Motherhood, Aging, and Memory in 'The Witching Hour'
item.description:
This interview features photographer Anastasia Sierra discussing her ongoing project 'The Witching Hour,' a cinematic and emotionally layered photographic series that explores motherhood, aging, and family dynamics. Sierra works with her young son and aging father as central figures, creating staged, dreamlike domestic scenes that blur the boundaries between reality, memory, and psychological space. The project evolved from her earlier work 'Bittersweet,' which focused on early motherhood and identity shifts during isolation, into a more complex narrative reflecting intergenerational coexistence under one roof.
Sierra describes photography as a deeply personal and intuitive medium that helps her process mixed emotions that are difficult to articulate in words. Her work is influenced by cinematic storytelling and surrealist aesthetics, with carefully composed images created in natural light within her home. The series reflects themes of emotional labor, care, distance, and the passage

item.title: Trump Posts Video Criticizing Michelle Obama While Highlighting JD Vance
item.description: On May 12, 2026, former President Donald Trump shared a video on his Truth Social platform that directly criticized former First Lady Michelle Obama while praising Vice President JD Vance. The video compared statements made by Obama regarding family life and parenting with remarks from Vance, suggesting a contrast in values and perspectives. Trump's post aimed to highlight differences between Obama's approach and what he described as Vance's more grounded view of family life. This is part of a long-standing pattern of personal attacks by Trump on both Michelle and Barack Obama, which have occurred throughout his political career, including his 2016 campaign, during his presidency, and after leaving office. Controversially, in February, Trump shared another post depicting the Obamas as animals, which sparked widespread condemnation despite his defense of the content. The video and post reflect Trump's ongoing strategy of criticizing Democratic figures while promoting allies, further fue

item.title: Fatal runway intrusion at Denver International Airport linked to suicide, officials confirm
item.description:
A tragic incident occurred at Denver International Airport when a man was struck and killed by a Frontier Airlines aircraft during its takeoff roll. Authorities have confirmed that the individual, identified as 41-year-old Michael Mott, had intentionally entered the restricted airfield area with the intention of taking his own life. The Denver Chief Medical Examiner ruled the manner of death a suicide, while local police continue to investigate the circumstances surrounding his actions.
The incident involved Frontier Airlines Flight 4345, an Airbus A321 carrying 224 passengers and seven crew members, which was departing for Los Angeles. During the takeoff sequence, the aircraft struck the man on the runway, causing an emergency situation onboard. The crew reported an engine fire and immediately halted the departure. Smoke filled parts of the cabin, prompting an emergency evacuation using slides. According to the airline, twelve passengers were injured during the evacuation, with five

item.title: White House rejects claims Trump fell asleep during Oval Office maternal health event
item.description:
During a maternal health event held in the Oval Office on Monday, President Donald Trump became the focus of attention after social media users and journalists suggested he appeared to fall asleep during the proceedings. The controversy began when Reuters national security correspondent Idrees Ali shared an image showing Trump with his eyes closed. In response, the White House rapid response account strongly denied the claim, stating that the president was simply blinking and criticizing the interpretation.
However, the explanation was quickly challenged online. Video clips circulated showing Trump with his eyes closed for approximately 19 seconds, which many users argued was far longer than a normal blink. The footage fueled speculation that the president had briefly fallen asleep during the event. The incident sparked widespread discussion on social media platforms, with commentators and political activists weighing in on both sides.
Some critics mocked the White House’s explanatio

item.title: Trump Organization’s Tbilisi Tower Project Raises Concerns Over Partners’ Alleged Russian Links
item.description:
The Trump Organization is moving forward with an international real estate project, Trump Tower Tbilisi, a planned 70-story luxury apartment building in Georgia that would become the tallest structure in the country. According to reporting cited in the article, the project involves partnerships with two companies that have alleged ties to Russian-linked figures, raising ethical and political concerns.
One of the partners, Archi Group, is a Tbilisi-based developer reportedly connected to Bidzina Ivanishvili, a powerful Georgian figure sanctioned by the United States. The company’s founder, Ilia Tsulaia, has political ties to Ivanishvili’s circle and previously benefited from government financing during a period when associates contributed to Ivanishvili’s political network. Although Tsulaia appeared on a list of potential sanctions targets, no formal sanctions were ultimately imposed.
The second partner, the Sapir Organization, is a U.S.-based real estate firm founded by Tamir Sapir,

item.title: Sen. Shaheen challenges Defense Secretary Hegseth over Iran conflict progress and sanctions policy in Senate hearing
item.description:
During a Senate Appropriations Committee hearing on Tuesday, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced sharp questioning from Senator Jeanne Shaheen regarding the ongoing conflict involving Iran and broader geopolitical consequences tied to sanctions policy. Shaheen argued that despite official rhetoric suggesting progress, the United States has not achieved decisive success in weakening Iran’s military capabilities or its role in regional instability.
Shaheen highlighted intelligence reports suggesting Russia is benefiting financially from reduced sanctions enforcement, allegedly earning billions through oil-related revenue. She warned that these funds may indirectly support Iran’s military production capacity, including drone manufacturing, which continues to play a role in ongoing conflicts such as the war in Ukraine. According to her remarks, Iranian-made Shahed drones remain relatively inexpensive to produce, enabling large-scale deployment supported by external financial flows.
Hegs

item.title: Katharine Hepburn's Childhood: Family, Activism, and Early Tragedy
item.description: Katharine Hepburn, born May 12, 1907, in Hartford, Connecticut, grew up in a progressive and intellectually vibrant upper-middle-class family that shaped her independent and strong-willed personality. She was the second of six children in a household led by socially active parents: Dr. Thomas Norval Hepburn, a urologist and advocate for public education on health issues, and Katharine Martha Houghton Hepburn, a feminist and birth control activist. From a young age, Katharine engaged in debates, participated in political rallies, and pursued athletic activities without gender constraints, excelling in swimming, tennis, and golf. Nicknamed 'Jimmy,' she embraced a tomboyish lifestyle, staging plays and demonstrating early theatrical talent. Her childhood, however, was marked by tragedy when she discovered her older brother Tom's apparent suicide in 1921. This event deeply affected her, leading to a period of withdrawal and private tutoring. Despite hardships, Hepburn credited her upbringi

item.title: Infinix Launches XBOOK B14 Laptop in the Philippines with Ryzen 7 and 14-inch Display
item.description: Infinix has officially launched its latest laptop, the XBOOK B14, in the Philippines. The device features a 14-inch IPS LCD display with a resolution of 1920 x 1200 pixels and a 16:10 aspect ratio, providing bright and clear visuals at up to 250 nits. Performance is powered by an AMD Ryzen 7 7735HS processor coupled with AMD Radeon 680M integrated graphics. The laptop comes equipped with 8GB DDR5 RAM and 512GB M.2 storage, offering a balance of speed and storage for everyday tasks. The XBOOK B14 includes a 55Wh battery with 65W USB-C Power Delivery charging, enabling quick charging and portability. Additional features include an HD webcam with a privacy shutter, dual microphones, stereo speakers with DTS Audio Processing, and Windows 11 Home as the operating system. The laptop also supports modern connectivity standards such as WiFi 6 and Bluetooth 5.2. Measuring 309.5 × 217.8 × 17.55 mm and weighing 1.18 kg, it is lightweight and portable. Infinix has priced the XBOOK B14 at PHP 24,99

item.title: Epstein survivor criticizes DOJ handling of file redactions during House Oversight Committee testimony
item.description:
During a House Oversight Committee hearing held in Palm Beach, Florida, a survivor of Jeffrey Epstein delivered emotional testimony criticizing the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) for what she described as severe mishandling of Epstein-related documents. The survivor, identified only as “Roza” to protect her privacy, became visibly emotional as she recounted how her identity had been exposed multiple times in publicly released records tied to the Epstein Files Transparency Act. According to her testimony, the DOJ failed to properly redact sensitive information, resulting in her name appearing more than 500 times in the released materials. She argued that this exposure has led to significant personal harm, including unwanted attention from media outlets and a constant sense of fear and vulnerability in her daily life.
Roza described her experience as a violation of trust and privacy, emphasizing that she had previously been protected under a pseudonymous identity. She accused authorit

item.title: Trump Administration's 2026 Counterterrorism Strategy Targets Violent Secular Political Groups and Antifa
item.description: The Trump administration released its 2026 U.S. Counterterrorism Strategy, emphasizing a focus on domestic threats alongside traditional ones like cartels and Islamist terror groups. The document prioritizes the identification and neutralization of 'violent secular political groups' described as having anti-American, radically pro-transgender, and anarchist ideologies. It calls for using constitutional law enforcement tools to map these groups, identify members, and disrupt their operations before they can commit violence. The strategy links these groups to international networks like Antifa and references incidents such as politically motivated attacks, including the assassination of conservative activist Charlie Kirk by an individual espousing extreme transgender ideologies. President Trump framed the approach as part of his 'America First' commitment to defend the nation from all enemies, foreign and domestic. This marks a shift in counterterrorism priorities, expanding beyond tradi

item.title: Wedge Pillow Can Help Improve Sleep, Sinus Relief, and Recovery
item.description: Sleep issues such as sleep apnea, snoring, acid reflux, and post-surgery discomfort can severely affect rest. Medical experts recommend elevating the head during sleep to improve airflow and reduce discomfort. A wedge pillow has gained attention for its supportive design, which includes a 24 x 24 x 7.5-inch incline, gel-infused memory foam for comfort on warm nights, and added features like a pillowcase and side pockets. Reviewers report improvements in sleep quality, sinus issues, acid reflux, and back support, noting that the wedge pillow can be used for side or back sleeping, or even under the legs for additional comfort. Although some users experienced minor initial odor or defective cases, the pillow generally provides consistent support and relief. Experts also suggest complementary methods for improving sleep and reducing snoring, including nasal strips, nasal irrigation devices, and staying hydrated with user-friendly water bottles. Overall, the wedge pillow appears to be a pra

item.title: BABYMONSTER Focuses on Music and World Tour Over Brand Collaborations
item.description: BABYMONSTER, a rising K-pop girl group under YG Entertainment, has chosen to temporarily decline multiple offers from global luxury brands. This decision aligns with the philosophy of YG Entertainment's chief producer, Yang Hyun Suk, who emphasizes that the group should focus primarily on music rather than external promotions. The management team aims to preserve the energy and creative potential of the group, ensuring that members—some of whom are underage—receive proper care and attention while maintaining a high standard of performance. BABYMONSTER recently released their mini-album CHOOM on May 4th, achieving impressive milestones such as topping the iTunes Worldwide Album Chart, securing number one on album charts in 19 regions, and breaking their own first-day sales record on Hanteo Chart with 387,871 copies sold. The group is preparing for an expansive world tour across Asia and Oceania, including major cities like Manila, Bangkok, Sydney, and Osaka, where they will make a rapid

item.title: Iran Saw Largest Increase in Jailed Writers Globally in 2025, Per PEN America Report
item.description: According to PEN America's 2025 Freedom to Write Index, Iran recorded the sharpest rise in imprisoned writers worldwide last year, jailing 43 authors—more than any other nation except China, which led with 119. This marked a significant escalation in Tehran's crackdown on dissent, surpassing previous years amid ongoing protests and regional tensions. The report highlights that Iranian authorities targeted writers linked to the Woman Life Freedom movement, women's rights advocacy, and criticism of mandatory hijab laws, with 15 female writers imprisoned—the highest globally. Overall, 401 writers were jailed across 44 countries in 2025, up from 375 the prior year, reflecting a 69% global increase over seven years. Saudi Arabia ranked third with 27 jailed writers, followed by Vietnam (24), Turkey (22), Israel (21), and Russia (18). In Iran, many detainees faced charges for peaceful expression, including anti-war sentiments and ethnic minority advocacy. PEN America urges immediate releases

item.title: Senator Criticizes Defense Secretary Hegseth Over Priorities Amid Iran War
item.description: During a Senate Appropriations Committee hearing on May 12, 2026, Senator Chris Coons (D-DE) publicly criticized U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth for focusing on domestic culture issues instead of military strategy and international conflicts. Coons highlighted the ongoing war in Iran and expressed concern that the administration is distracted by internal controversies, including book bans, removing DEI initiatives, anti-vaccine positions, and personnel changes in the military. He specifically called out the firing of the 44-year Army Chief of Staff and the dismissal of the Secretary of the Navy amid critical operations, warning these actions could undermine morale and focus. Additionally, Coons criticized President Donald Trump's attention to the construction of a new White House ballroom and the proposed $1.5 trillion budget that includes new battleships, arguing that modern warfare requires smaller, faster, more distributed, and autonomous capabilities rather than traditional

item.title: U.S. Nationals from Hantavirus-Affected Cruise Ship Under Quarantine and Monitoring
item.description: Seventeen Americans and one dual British-U.S. citizen were evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship after a hantavirus outbreak and arrived in the U.S. on May 11, 2026. One passenger tested mildly positive for the Andes strain, responsible for the outbreak, while another began showing symptoms. Both were transported in specialized biocontainment units as a precaution. Sixteen passengers were sent to the University of Nebraska Medical Center for monitoring—15 to the National Quarantine Unit and one to a biocontainment unit. Additional passengers were flown to Atlanta for further assessment. The virus outbreak on the ship has caused at least ten confirmed or suspected cases, including three deaths. International authorities coordinated a careful disembarkation process, with passengers evacuated by nationality, monitored for symptoms, and kept isolated from local populations. The WHO and CDC maintain that the risk to the general public remains low. Passengers from other countries, includ

item.title: Pete Davidson Makes Controversial Joke About Kanye West During Kevin Hart Roast
item.description: During a comedy roast for Kevin Hart at the Kia Forum in Los Angeles, Pete Davidson made headlines by targeting several celebrities, including the late Charlie Kirk, Tony Hinchcliffe, Lizzo, and Kanye West. Davidson referenced his ongoing feud with Kanye, jokingly calling him a 'gay Nazi' while poking fun at Kanye's past controversial embrace of Nazi symbolism and comments, which he later apologized for in a full-page Wall Street Journal ad. The joke also alludes to the highly publicized tension stemming from Davidson's previous short relationship with Kim Kardashian, Kanye's ex-wife. Kanye had mocked Davidson online with a derogatory nickname and videos suggesting violence. The comedy remark has sparked curiosity over how Kanye will respond, especially considering his current marriage to Bianca Censori. The roast was filled with sharp humor, but Davidson’s remarks about Kanye stand out as the most provocative moment of the night.

item.title: DC Weather Update: Pleasant Tuesday, Rain Wednesday, and Warmer Temperatures Later in the Week
item.description: The Capital Weather Gang provides an updated forecast for Washington, D.C., for the week ahead. Tuesday will experience excellent weather with afternoon highs reaching the upper 60s to low 70s and light winds, making it an ideal day for outdoor activities. Meteorologists predict a shift in weather conditions by Wednesday, with increasing chances of rain that may affect commuting and outdoor plans. Following midweek precipitation, temperatures are expected to rise later in the week, bringing warmer conditions and a pleasant end to the workweek. Residents are advised to monitor daily updates, as weather patterns can change rapidly, particularly in the spring season. Overall, the forecast highlights a brief mix of sun, rain, and warmth, giving locals a chance to enjoy favorable weather before midweek rain arrives.

item.title: 1908 Cartercar Model D Roadster: Byron Carter's Innovative Friction-Drive Automobile
item.description: The 1908 Cartercar Model D Roadster represented a unique chapter in early automotive history thanks to its innovative friction drive transmission system, invented by Byron Carter. Marketed as offering 'A Thousand Speeds,' the gearless vehicle used two perpendicular friction disks to transfer power, eliminating many common issues of the era such as slipping clutches, stripped gears, broken universal joints, and twisting shaft drives. The friction drive featured replaceable paper fiber rims that could be renewed every 4,000 miles for around five dollars, making maintenance more affordable than traditional geared transmissions. Visually striking, the Model D featured prominent brass Rushmore Searchlight headlamps, Corcoran brass side and taillamps, and a Rushmore carbide generator positioned beside the driver's seat. It offered comfortable diamond-pattern buttoned black leather seats and a simple folding black canvas top, though it lacked a windshield. By 1908, the company had sold approx

item.title: Engineered Algae Shows Potential to Remove Microplastics and Clean Wastewater
item.description: Researchers at the University of Missouri have developed a genetically engineered strain of algae that could help remove microplastics from water sources, including drinking water. Led by Professor Susie Dai, the algae produce limonene, a natural orange-scented oil that makes the algae water-repellent. This property allows microplastics, which are also hydrophobic, to adhere to the algae, forming clumps that sink and can be easily collected. Beyond capturing microplastics, the algae can grow in wastewater, absorbing excess nutrients and contributing to overall water purification. Dai envisions recycling the collected microplastics into safer bioplastics, such as composite plastic films, creating a closed-loop system that addresses pollution while producing useful materials. Her team already operates a 100-liter bioreactor named 'Shrek' to grow algae and process industrial flue gas, and she aims to scale the system for wastewater treatment. Although still in early stages, this research

item.title: The Plaster Casts of Pompeii: How Archaeologists Preserved the Final Moments of Vesuvius Victims
item.description: The famous figures from Pompeii, often mistaken for petrified human bodies, are actually plaster casts made by archaeologists. When Mount Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD, it buried the Roman city under layers of volcanic ash and pumice that hardened around the victims. As soft tissues decomposed over centuries, they left behind hollow cavities in the solidified ash. In 1863, Giuseppe Fiorelli developed an innovative technique to fill these voids with liquid plaster. Once hardened, the surrounding material was removed, revealing detailed replicas of the victims in their final poses, complete with expressions of agony, clothing folds, and body positions. The original skeletal remains rest inside many of these plaster shells, as confirmed by modern CT scans and DNA analysis. These analyses have also corrected some assumptions, such as revealing that a famous 'mother and child' embrace actually involved a man. Over 1,000 victims have been identified, but only around 100 casts were created; other

item.title: Trump Denies Republican Warnings Against Military Action in Cuba
item.description: President Donald Trump publicly denied reports that Senate Republicans had cautioned him against ordering military strikes on Cuba. The 79-year-old president issued a 40-word statement on Truth Social, asserting that no Republican had spoken to him about Cuba and describing the nation as 'failed.' The remarks came ahead of his departure to China for discussions with President Xi Jinping. The denial appears to respond to a report by The Hill, which claimed that Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD) and other GOP senators had expressed reservations about potential military action while the U.S. is focused on tensions with Iran. Senators such as James Lankford (R-OK) and Shelley Moore Capito (R-WV) emphasized economic pressure and Iran as priorities over Cuba, despite earlier administration statements suggesting possible military maneuvers near Havana. Trump has also hinted at deploying an aircraft carrier group near Cuba, but GOP support for such action remains tepid. The article high

item.title: New CNN Poll Shows Declining Public Confidence in Trump’s Economic Leadership
item.description:
A new CNN poll conducted by SSRS suggests growing public dissatisfaction with President Donald Trump’s handling of the U.S. economy, an issue that has historically been one of his strongest political advantages. According to the survey, 77 percent of Americans—including a notable share of Republicans—believe Trump’s policies are contributing to the rising cost of living. His economic approval rating has dropped to 30 percent, marking the lowest point of his political career.
CNN anchor Kate Bolduan emphasized the depth of concern among Americans, noting that 73 percent describe current economic conditions as poor. Financial anxiety appears widespread, with many Americans struggling to manage everyday expenses. The poll indicates that roughly two-thirds of respondents would be unable to comfortably cover an unexpected $1,000 expense, highlighting concerns about financial stability and household resilience.
CNN political director David Chalian added context by pointing out that economi

item.title: Senator Accuses Pentagon of Failing to Provide Required Congressional Briefings
item.description: During a House Appropriations Subcommittee hearing on President Trump's FY2027 defense budget, U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth faced tough questions regarding military transparency. Senator Jack Reed (D-RI), ranking member of the Senate Armed Services Committee, criticized the Pentagon for failing to provide required briefings to Congress, stating that the Department of Defense has not been sharing meaningful information with either party. Reed emphasized that the Pentagon's refusal violates legal requirements for reporting within specified timeframes. He warned that if Democrats regain control of the Senate in November, the Armed Services Committee would likely investigate recent high-profile Pentagon dismissals. In response to inquiries about the costs of the ongoing conflict in Iran, Hegseth stated that the Department would release information when it is relevant and legally required. This exchange highlights ongoing tensions between Congress and the Department of Defense ove

item.title: Emma de Burgh: Pioneering Tattoo Art in 19th Century America
item.description: Emma de Burgh, born in 1860, was a renowned American circus performer known for her elaborate back tattoo, which depicted Leonardo da Vinci's 'The Last Supper.' Created by the celebrated tattoo artist Samuel O’Reilly in New York City, her artwork spanned her shoulders and included religious text like 'The Golden Rule' and 'Love One Another.' Many of the tattoos were likely done by hand, as this was around the time O'Reilly patented the electric tattoo machine in 1891. During the Victorian era, tattoos were considered scandalous, but Emma and her husband, Frank de Burgh, intentionally selected religious and patriotic motifs to project respectability. The couple gained international attention, including in Britain, where Emma falsely claimed her tattoos commemorated Queen Victoria’s Golden Jubilee. Both Emma and Frank performed together, with Frank’s back featuring a large Crucifixion tattoo, and they famously tattooed each other’s names as symbols of their love. Pre-Raphaelite painter E

item.title: A Quiet Moment of Navajo Family Life Captured in 1948
item.description: In 1948, photographer Leonard McCombe captured a serene moment on the Navajo Nation in Arizona, showing a Diné woman brushing her husband's long hair while their child sat nearby. The man in the photograph was the son of Yellowsalt, belonging to a family of sheep herders. This photograph was part of McCombe's LIFE Magazine essay titled “A Country Within a Country,” which aimed to depict the everyday realities of Navajo life during a period of social and economic transition. In Diné culture, long hair has traditionally carried deep significance, representing identity, strength, family ties, and harmony. Hair grooming rituals often held cultural and spiritual meaning, passed down through generations. During the mid-20th century, Navajo families were navigating the pressures of modernization, assimilation policies, and economic hardship resulting from decades of displacement and federal control over tribal lands. While this man adhered to the tradition of wearing long hair, younger member

item.title: Food Industry Uses 'Make America Healthy Again' Movement to Push Weaker Federal Regulations
item.description: The processed food industry is leveraging the 'Make America Healthy Again' (MAHA) movement to lobby for federal standards that could undercut stricter state-level regulations on food additives, labeling, and school nutrition. While the Trump administration encourages voluntary reductions in synthetic dyes and limits on marketing to children, industry lobbyists are quietly advocating on Capitol Hill to establish weaker national standards. Experts, including Georgetown law professor Lawrence Gostin, warn that this maneuver prioritizes corporate interests over children's health. The industry has spent a record $113 million on lobbying since President Trump's return to office, with major companies like Coca-Cola and PepsiCo leading the effort. Congressional actions have aligned with these corporate goals, blocking amendments that would restrict SNAP purchases of sugary drinks and suppressing a long-delayed FTC report on junk food marketing to children. Consumer advocates fear that a federa

item.title: Regular Participation in Arts and Cultural Activities May Slow Biological Aging
item.description: A recent study from University College London has found that actively engaging in arts and cultural activities, such as singing, painting, or visiting galleries and museums, may slow the pace of biological aging. The research, published in the journal Innovation in Aging, used blood tests and survey data from 3,556 adults in the UK Household Longitudinal Study to assess biological age and the rate of aging. The study found that individuals who participated in arts activities weekly experienced a 4% slower aging process, while monthly engagement slowed aging by 3%. Another assessment method showed weekly participants were on average biologically one year younger than those who rarely engaged, surpassing the benefits seen from weekly exercise, which showed a six-month difference. Lead researcher Prof. Daisy Fancourt emphasized that arts engagement has measurable health effects at a biological level and suggested it should be considered a health-promoting behavior similar to physical exer

item.title: Iron-60 in Antarctic Ice Reveals Solar System's Passage Through Interstellar Cloud
item.description: Researchers studying Antarctic ice cores have discovered traces of the rare iron isotope iron-60, which provides insights into Earth's movement through the cosmos. Iron-60 is produced in extreme events like supernova explosions and cannot form naturally on Earth in significant amounts. The team, led by nuclear astrophysicist Dominik Koll, found iron-60 in ice cores dating from 40,000 to 81,000 years ago, as well as in recent snowfall, indicating the Solar System has been passing through a cloud of supernova debris. By analyzing 295 kilograms of Antarctic ice, the researchers identified variations in iron-60 concentrations, suggesting that the Local Interstellar Cloud through which the Solar System moves has regions of differing density. This ice core record effectively serves as a 'flight log' of Earth's journey through this cloud over the past 80,000 years. The study supports the idea that the Local Interstellar Cloud may be composed of material from supernova events and highlights th

item.title: Gena Rowlands: Celebrating the Life and Legacy of an Independent Film Icon
item.description: Gena Rowlands (1930–2024) was a monumental figure in American cinema, celebrated for her intense and emotionally raw performances. She is best remembered for her groundbreaking collaborations with director John Cassavetes, her husband, in films such as 'A Woman Under the Influence' (1974) and 'Gloria' (1980). Rowlands' acting style was distinguished by its honesty and depth, portraying complex and vulnerable women in a way that resonated deeply with audiences. While some later generations might recognize her for her role in 'The Notebook,' her true impact lies in pioneering independent film and redefining the scope of female characters on screen. Over a career spanning decades, she earned two Academy Award nominations and received an Honorary Oscar later in life, establishing her as an “actor’s actor.” These vintage portraits of Rowlands highlight her extraordinary range, intensity, and enduring presence, capturing the essence of a woman whose work continues to influence modern filmmak

item.title: Political Parties Intensify Efforts to Redraw Congressional Districts
item.description: The ongoing fight over congressional redistricting has escalated into a highly visible political battle. Both Republican and Democratic parties are actively attempting to redraw House district lines to gain electoral advantages. Analysts note that these efforts are becoming increasingly aggressive, with strategies that could significantly influence upcoming elections. One prominent example occurred in Texas, where former President Donald Trump publicly highlighted a plan to add five congressional seats, citing his strong voter support in the state as justification. The redistricting process, often seen as a technical and administrative task, has now become a focal point for partisan strategy, demonstrating how electoral maps can shape political power. Experts warn that such maneuvers, while legal in many cases, can erode public trust in the democratic process if perceived as overtly manipulative. As the debate continues, it underscores the tension between legal redistricting authority

item.title: John Engstead and the Rise of California-Style Fashion Photography in the 1940s
item.description:
John Engstead (1909–1983) was a defining figure in Hollywood’s Golden Age photography, known for reshaping the visual language of fashion and celebrity portraiture. Unlike many of his contemporaries who relied heavily on controlled studio environments, Engstead embraced natural light and outdoor settings, particularly the bright, sunlit landscapes of Southern California. This approach allowed him to create images that felt both polished and relaxed, helping to establish what became known as the “California Look.”
His work stood out for its balance between elegance and accessibility. Engstead photographed well-known figures such as Lauren Bacall, Ella Raines, Slim Keith, and June Vincent, presenting them in a way that highlighted both their glamour and their natural presence. His images often emphasized fluid movement, soft textures, and luminous skin tones, with careful attention to how fabrics interacted with light. These qualities gave his photographs a sense of vitality that distin

item.description: Unable to generate a summary of the news article because the content at the provided WSJ URL could not be accessed. The search results only showed snippets from related live coverage pages about the CPI inflation report and Iran conflict affecting oil prices, but did not include the full article about the Abu Dhabi gas company. To complete this request, direct access to the Wall Street Journal article would be required, which typically requires a subscription. The search results indicate the article is related to an Abu Dhabi gas company being impacted by war details, likely in the context of the U.S.-Iran standoff mentioned in related coverage from May 12, 2026.

item.title: Patricia Howard Explores Irish Heritage Through Landscapes and Objects
item.description: Patricia Howard's project 'Unknown Ancestors' investigates a largely unrecorded period of Irish history, particularly the Great Famine, a time when photography was scarce. Growing up, Howard had limited knowledge of her family's past due to her father's reluctance to discuss their Irish heritage, influenced by societal stigma. During a residency in County Kerry, she photographed rural landscapes using simple, restrained methods, embracing the environment's limitations. These images were later transformed into cyanotype prints on handmade semi-transparent paper, a process that emphasizes both the land and its historical absence. Howard also incorporated natural materials like wool and gorse flowers, as well as personal and domestic objects such as clothing, hair, spoons, and rosaries, to evoke the lives of those who came before her. Many of the prints include embroidery, connecting the work to a lineage of women and reflecting her own childhood experiences. Through these careful, tactil

item.title: Sen. John Curtis Considers 2028 Utah Gubernatorial Run Amid Senate Frustrations
item.description: Senator John Curtis of Utah, a moderate Republican, is exploring a potential run for governor in 2028 just 16 months after starting his term in the U.S. Senate. Curtis’ inner circle has been quietly gauging support among donors and allies, encouraging them to hold off on backing other gubernatorial candidates. His chief of staff emphasized that Curtis will serve where Utah citizens want him to, highlighting his pragmatic approach and preference for executive roles, like his time as Provo’s mayor. The timing of Curtis’ considerations coincides with former Rep. Jason Chaffetz attempting to clear the field for a gubernatorial bid. Curtis’ political history includes a reputation as a dealmaker, a China hawk, and a supporter of conservation initiatives. Although he has occasionally opposed Trump, he voted in line with the former president in 2025. Curtis is using personal retreats and a planned 250-mile walk across Utah to reflect on his political future. While some Utah Republicans support

item.title: Local Families Provide Care and Relief for Chernobyl-Affected Children
item.description: On the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster, reflections turned to personal stories of aid and compassion. In the years following the 1986 meltdown, Belarusian and Ukrainian doctors issued urgent appeals to remove children from highly radioactive environments to prevent further health damage. One initiative, Chernobyl Children International, founded by Adi Roche, facilitated programs that brought affected children to Ireland for medical treatment and respite. Northern Ireland later established similar programs. Between 1994 and 2014, the Chernobyl's Children Appeal brought 3,400 children to Northern Ireland. Among the volunteers, Mairead Burke from Randalstown hosted two girls, Marina and Galina, offering them a safe home, nutritious meals, and daily activities. Local communities contributed through donations, organized outings, and gifts, helping the children experience a normal and joyful life despite their hardships. The program highlighted cultural exchanges, showing the star

item.title: High Consumption of Ultra-Processed Foods Increases Cardiovascular and Mortality Risks, Study Finds
item.description: A major new study published in the European Heart Journal has found strong associations between high consumption of ultra-processed foods and serious health risks, including early death and cardiovascular disease. Ultra-processed foods, such as fast foods, packaged snacks, soda, and instant noodles, are industrially manufactured with ingredients rarely used in home cooking. They are often high in sugar, salt, and unhealthy fats while lacking fiber and essential nutrients. Analysis of existing research indicates that individuals who consume the most ultra-processed foods have up to a 19% higher risk of heart disease, a 13% increased risk of atrial fibrillation, and up to a 65% higher risk of cardiovascular-related death compared to those who consume the least. The study highlights multiple pathways through which these foods affect health, including promoting weight gain, altering blood sugar and fat levels, raising blood pressure, and potentially impacting gut health and appetite regula

item.title: Trump Administration Officials Prioritize Personal Promotion Over Public Service
item.description: The article highlights a pattern of self-promotion among several officials in the Trump administration, illustrating a disregard for their official responsibilities and the American public. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy is featured in a corporate-sponsored family road trip YouTube docuseries while companies regulated by his department fund the trip. This occurs amid rising gas prices, airline industry failures, and unpaid federal workers. The piece also mentions former DHS Secretary Kristi Noem's $220 million taxpayer-funded ad campaign, RFK Jr.'s bizarre fitness video with Kid Rock, FBI Director Kash Patel distributing personalized bourbon while drinking affects agency operations, and Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth posting workout videos rather than focusing on Pentagon duties. The overarching critique is that these officials treat government roles as platforms for personal visibility rather than public service, contrasting sharply with the sacrifices ordinary Americans face

item.title: Anne Baxter: Career Highlights and Iconic Roles of the 1950s
item.description: Anne Baxter (1923–1985) was a distinguished American actress whose career spanned Hollywood films, Broadway, and television. The 1950s were a defining decade for her, as she transitioned from youthful roles of the 1940s into more complex and versatile leading characters. Her breakout performance during this period was as Eve Harrington in 'All About Eve' (1950), where she skillfully portrayed a seemingly innocent fan who manipulates a Broadway star, earning her an Academy Award nomination for Best Actress and establishing her as a commanding presence on screen. Throughout the decade, Baxter avoided typecasting by exploring various genres, including film noir, Westerns, and large-scale Technicolor productions. In 1953, she starred in Alfred Hitchcock’s 'I Confess,' demonstrating her ability to convey psychological tension. She also delivered a visually iconic performance as Nefretiri in Cecil B. DeMille’s 'The Ten Commandments' (1956), leaving a lasting impression on pop culture. By the

item.title: Color Portraits of British 8th Army Soldiers Serving in Italy, April 1944
item.description: In April 1944, War Office official photographer Captain A.R. Tanner captured a series of striking color photographs showcasing soldiers of the British 8th Army at their main headquarters in the San Angelo area of Italy. These images provide a vivid, personal look at the diverse roles and backgrounds of the men supporting the Allied campaign during World War II. The portraits highlight everyday duties and the human side of the war effort. Featured individuals include Corporal M. Smith of the Duke of Cornwall's Light Infantry, a former metal polisher from Birmingham holding his Thompson submachine gun. He had served in North Africa and Egypt before joining the fighting north of Cassino and along the Garigliano River. Signalman A. Johnson, a former bus conductor from Mill Hill, is shown with a reel of wire after extensive service from Egypt through Sicily to Italy. Private W.A. Bowen of the Army Catering Corps, previously a painter and decorator from South Wales, prepares potatoes after c

item.title: Americans Across the Political Spectrum Agree on What Makes a Good Teacher
item.description: Despite highly politicized debates surrounding education in the United States, a recent study reveals that Americans, regardless of political affiliation, share surprisingly similar views on what constitutes a very good teacher. Researchers surveyed over 2,000 adults from 2020 to 2025, asking them to describe their most effective teachers. Respondents consistently highlighted the importance of relationships, individualized support, and making learning relevant, rather than focusing on strict discipline, competition, or covering extensive material. Teachers surveyed in Arizona and California echoed these priorities, emphasizing both subject mastery and student engagement. A further experiment tested whether political labeling affected perceptions: when descriptions of good teaching were tagged as endorsed by a political party, agreement slightly decreased, especially among Republicans. Nevertheless, nearly two-thirds of participants maintained cross-partisan consensus. The study demonst

item.title: Sen. Cornyn Signals Support for Temporary Gas Tax Suspension Following Trump's Endorsement of Opponent's Proposal
item.description: Senator John Cornyn of Texas initially criticized a proposal from his Democratic challenger, James Talarico, to suspend the federal gas tax amid rising fuel prices caused by conflict in Iran.
